title: Pesticide Free Garden
lyrics:
How many kinds of wild flowers grow
In a pesticide-free gar-den?
We'll tell you now of some that we know
Those we miss you'll surely par-don.
Chicory and strawberry, buttercups and dais-y,
Queen Anne's Lace and Creeping Charlie
Make a rainbow of color all through the year
In a pesticide-free gar-den.

How many kinds of grass can you spy
In a pesticide-free gar-den
With roots reaching deep and blades growing high,
In a pesticide-free gar-den?
Timothy, Kentucky Blue, Bent and Rye and Fes-cue,
Indian and Blue Stem, Sweet Vernal too,
With clover in the seed to help meet every need
In a pesticide free gar-den.

How many kinds of creatures will come
To a pesticide-free gar-den?
With a trill and a squawk, a whirr and a hum
In a pesticide-free gar-den.
Bumblebees and butterflies, ladybugs and dragonflies,
Warblers and grosbeaks, robins, chickadees
Cedar waxwings and bluejays, cardinals and wrens
They will love your healthy gar-den.

What will it take for all of us to make
Our own pesticide-free gar-den.
If we can see we've made a big mistake
We-ll be asking nature's par-don.
Find another safer way, to make a place for kids to play,
Working and waiting, welcoming the day,
When we all can delight in the sounds and the sights
Of a pesticide-free gar-den!

title: Bombs on Fallujah!
lyrics:
<strong>Glory<strong>, glory Hallelujah!We’re <strong>dropping<strong> bombs on poor Fallujah!
Our <strong>bombs<strong> will bring democracy and voting rights for all,
Just <strong>like <strong>we have at home.

We <strong>know<strong> that God is on our side and that our way is right,
And <strong>so<strong> we drop our bombs on you so that you’ll see the light.
We <strong>want<strong> you to be just like us, so why put up a fight?
Our <strong>bombs<strong> will set you free!

<strong>Glory<strong>, glory Hallelujah!
If <strong>you’re<strong> not with us we say “Screw ya!”
We’ll <strong>kill <strong>you all until you have democracy like ours!
Our <strong>bombs<strong> will set you free!

We <strong>know <strong>that you’ll be grateful when our bombing raids are done,
And <strong>those<strong> of you still left alive will bless us everyone,
And<strong> vote<strong> the way we want you to – now won’t that be such fun?
Our <strong>bombs<strong> will set you free!

<strong>Glory,<strong> glory Hallelujah!
We’re <strong>bringin’ <strong>democracy right to ya!
So <strong>just <strong>put down your guns and never mind your sovereignty --
Our <strong>bombs <strong>will set you free!

We’ll <strong>conquer<strong> every nation state that doesn’t stand with us
And <strong>there’s<strong> no use in putting up a protest or a fuss --
We’ve <strong>gone<strong> and killed our Bill of Rights so that you could be free.
No <strong>one<strong> dare disagree!

[Repeat first chorus]

title: Anthem for Peace
lyrics:
O-oh, say can you see, we have no moral rightto continue these wars, hear the innocents screaming,
who-ose homes have been bombed into burial sites.
On the news we have watched the result of the scheming.

To all the war games, we say “not in our names!”
International law is ignored  to our shame.
Oh say , will we keep fighting and ma-aking it all wo-orse,
‘Til the whole world is in flames, and America cursed?

title: Song for Delray Beach: The “Village by the Sea”
lyrics:
<strong>We’re<strong> the Raging Grannies and we’re singing right out loudTo <strong>say<strong> six-story buildings in Delray can’t be allowed.
Let’s<strong> slow<strong> the growth and keep our town as sweet as it is now –
Our village by the sea.

CHORUS:
Bigger isn’t always better,
Bigger isn’t always better.
If <strong>we <strong>all join together we can save our Delray Beach –
Our village by the sea.

It <strong>isn’t<strong> right that those with cash can come into our town
And <strong>break <strong>up ethnic neighborhoods and tear old buildings down.
We <strong>want<strong> to stay the way we are – the greatest place around –
Our village by the sea.
CHORUS

We <strong>don't <strong>want big tall buildings overshadowing our streets.
We <strong>need <strong>commissioners who understand that small is sweet.
We <strong>need <strong>a master plan that slows the growth so we can save
Our village by the sea.
CHORUS

title: Health Concerns
lyrics:
Well I know I’m past my prime but  I’m feelin’ pretty fine,I’m finally finished with the Curse,
And though my belly’s saggin’ and my butt is really draggin’
I didn’t think it could get much worse.

I load on the sun screen and I’ve cut back on caffeine,
I always watch my cholesterol
I walk two miles a day and watch how much I weigh
The aging process to forestall.

	But now the EPA, has a lot to say,
	about things I just don’t understand,
	like the ozone layer and pollutants in the air,
	they talk about contaminated land.
Ooooh...

I’ve been havin’ dreams about sewage in the streams
so bottled water’s all I drink,
and in my dreams I’m chased by ghosts of toxic wastes
and I’m afraid to put my dishes in the sink!

And I don’t eat any dish that’s made with tuna fish
‘cause it just might contain mercury,
and I bought a pocket flask and a surplus gas mask
and I thought I’d be as safe as I can be.

	But now I always sneeze and I’m starting to wheeze,
	I worry about Mad Cow Disease,
	no matter what I try, I’m probably gonna die
	and the EPA, it just agrees..
Ooooh...

	no matter what I try, I’m probably gonna die
	and the EPA, it just agrees..

title: Recruiters Lie
lyrics:
<strong>We're<strong> the Raging Grannies!We’re <strong>mad<strong> as mad can be!
Re<strong>cruit<strong>ers lie, our children die;
That’s <strong>not<strong> how it should be!

Our <strong>kids <strong>are told that they’ll get jobs
And <strong>money<strong> for college too,
But <strong>you<strong> can bet that what they’ll get
Is <strong>just<strong> the royal screw.

They <strong>send<strong> our kids to die in war
But <strong>what<strong> have they achieved?
They <strong>say <strong>they’re fighting terrorists
But <strong>we<strong> won’t be deceived.

<strong>Hal<strong>liburton’s getting rich.
It’s <strong>really<strong> quite uncouth
To <strong>profit<strong> from war. We say: No more!
It’s <strong>time<strong> we heard the truth!

Let’s <strong>give<strong> our kids the jobs they need
So<strong> they <strong>don’t have to go!
And <strong>when <strong>recruiters hunt them down
<strong>They<strong> can all say NO!

We’re <strong>here <strong>to say we want our kids
To <strong>grow<strong> up safe and sound.
Not <strong>get <strong>blown up by a roadside bomb
<strong>Half<strong> the world around.

<strong>War<strong> is not the answer,
And <strong>so <strong>we’re here to say:
We’ve<strong> had<strong> enough, its time to get t<strong>ough <strong>–
(sweetly) The <strong>sweet <strong>old Granny way.
tune: 99 Bottles of Beer on the Wall

title: Her Song Lives On (Revised Version)
lyrics:
Does her song die
When a Gran is gone?
Or does her melody live on?
I think when she’s no longer bound
To this troubled Earth
Her song lives on.

Her songs helped quench
The flames of war,
And stilled the tyrants evermore.
And through the strife we hear her still,
And so we know
Her song glives on.

The songs she sang
While with us here,
Still bring a smile, a laugh, a tear…
And those she touched must surely know
’Though she is gone,
Her song lives on.
songwriter: Rose DeShaw, Laureen Martin, Vicki Ryder

title: Anthem for Clean Air
lyrics:
O-oh, say can you see, through the smog and the blight,
the rampant release of industrial waste streaming?
Through our polluted air,
strong enough to ignite,
oxygen-starved,
we cough and choke while we’re dreaming
of the days of clean air,
before we were aware
of foul poisons produced by big industry’s affairs.
Oh, say, will the-ee EPA continue to ignore and wa-aive
Blame on those who are at fault
who’ll send us all to our graves?

title: Get Out and Vote
lyrics: Last night some new thoughts did surround me When thinking of heroes of old, What they fought for does really astound me, They were so brave and so bold. The men said that women weren’t able To think or to make up their minds So men would make all the decisions And leave all the women behind. REFRAIN: Rise up, sing out On matters that matter to you and me! Sign up! Get out and vote And we’ll make a difference, you’ll see! Some brave women in our communities Refused to be quiet and meek They struggled to gain opportunities To work and to vote and to speak. Our Susan B. Anth’ny and others, They spoke and they marched and they wrote, Saying women must be treated fairly And they must be allowed to vote! REFRAIN Now many long years have transpired Since brave women won us a voice They worked and they struggled together, So we’d have a vote and a choice. REFRAIN
tune: My Bonnie Lies Over the Ocean

title: CIA Hideaway
lyrics:
CIA HIDEAWAY(Tune: “Hernando’s Hideaway ”)

There is a dark secluded place
A place you vanish with no trace
identity they will erase
It’s run....by the American CIA! (CIA?)

They bug your phone and TV set
they monitor you on the internet
and though you’ve nothing to regret
you’re surveilled by the CIA.... (Oy vey!)

(Interlude)

(kazoos): Da da, da da, da da, da da da da da da)
     To mid-east dictatorships that you don’t even know
(kazoos): Da da, da da, da da, da da da da da da)
      Seven-thirty-sevens take you where you’ll undergo
(kazoos): La la, La la, la la, la la la la la la)
      imprisonment, privation, torture and much woe
      you won’t be free...
      to disagree
      or cop a ple-ea.... (slow)

Our government says “Way to go!”
We’ll find out everything you know
You’ll be black and blue from head to toe
And no one will blame the CIA! (No way!)

So always check your TV set
don’t give them reason to suspect
email in code or you’ll regret ....
You’re fin-gered by the CIA! (Ole!)

title: Gulf Wars
lyrics:
GULF WARS (tune“The Sloop John B”)
We fought in the first Gulf War,
invaded a foreign shore
in a coalition approved by the U.N.
Missiles falling all night,
not much of a fight,
well, we came and conquered,
watched it on CNN

     Petty and wasteful war.
     What were we fighting for?
     Kuwaiti freedom from Saddam Hussein
     and of course all the oil
     in their desert soil. (Yeah, yeah)
     The quick U.S. slam dunk
     let most troops come back home.

But depleted uranium
in missiles for Desert Storm,
spread radioactive dust throughout the land.
Poison out of the sky,
many thousands will die,
“Dante’s Inferno”
they cannot withstand.

     A genocidal war,
     what were we trying for?
     Testing our own weapons of mass destruction,
     never used before
     in case of world war. (Yeah, yeah)
     We were so successful,
     vets got Gulf War Syndrome!.

Now we’re still in Iraq,
the neocons sent us back
to keep the whole world safe from WMD,
not to mention the oil,
it makes my blood boil....
The U.S. is drowning
in this lunacy.

     Imperialistic war,
     what are we fighting for?
     Hidden Iraqi weapons of mass destruction
     that never were there....
     lies from thin air.... (Yeah, yeah)
     We’ve got to stop the fighting,
     and bring the troops home!

BRING THEM HOME NOW!!

title: Walking In A Voter Wonderland
lyrics:
Phone bells ring, are you listening?Do you hear voters bristling?
It's all the same game, in Diebold's name,
Welcome to the voters' wonderland.

Early on, they've conspired, to fix, who will be fired
And just who will win - they've programmed it in,
Wake up to a voter's wonderland.

In the polling station there are boxes
For your ballot - you have marked it well
Carefully slipped it in the counting gismo
But what is counted you can never tell.

So tell the House - quit the stalling
No paper trail is appalling
Incumbents want to win, but that is a sin
Make an honest Voter Wonderland.

title: Unimpeachable
lyrics:
Unimpeachable, that’s what I amUnimpeachable, don’t give a damn
There’s no way that you can get to me
What you think don’t mean a heck to me

Never before has someone been more
Unimpeachable, I’m glad to say.
And furthermore
That’s how I’ll stay
Here’s why, darlings, I’m invincible
All you slobs are so convince-able, to
Think that I am unimpeachable too!

title: Hey, Dude!
lyrics:
HEY DUDE (tune: Hey Jude)
Hey Dude, don’t bomb Iran
It’s your worst idea yet, Si-i-ir!
You already got us all hung up in Iraq,
Our future the-ere’s...not looking better

Hey Dude, we know the plan
Fox News spin makes us all sweat, Si-i-ir....
With new stories of WMDs
Media hy-ype’s ....all that we get, Sir!

        Into another lion’s den, hey Dude, again???
        The only reason for this new wa-arfare
        Is you can’t win the war that we’re already in
         As war chief you’ve been... a total fai-ailure!
La-la-la-la, la-la, la-la ...

Hey Dude, there’s much worse threats....
Iran might be able to make one nuke, Si-i-ir....
We’ve twenty thousand in our own arsenal
There’s no-o rea-son...for us to spoooook, Sir!

       So before you invade again, hey Dude, begin
       to think of our lost four thousand so-oldiers,
       Your botched war and the destruction in Iraq
       And all the kids who’ll grow no o-older....
La-la-la-la, la-la, la-la
Hey Dude, you done us wrong
We’re all past fed up, you can bet, Si-i-ir...
We don’t want war with Ahmaddinejad
Just so you and Chen-ey can feel better!

Better, better, better, better, better....WHOA!
No, No, No-no-no-no-no, no-no-no-no-no, Hey Dude!
No, No, No-no-no-no-no, no-no-no-no-no, Hey Dude......

title: If You Don't Have Soldiers
lyrics:
If you don't have sailors, you cannot sailIf you don't have jailers, you cannot jail
If you don't have soldiers, you cannot have war
So tell me my brothers, what we're waiting for?

We cannot shoot missiles, if missiles aren't made
We work in those factories, from need to be paid
We serve in the army - there's no other job
Except Halliburton, and others who rob.

If Wall Street's your address and you cannot trade
You'll find the slave labor, or go and invade
We'll not be the small pawns, in this terrible game
Lie down on the chess board, refuse to be tame.

If THEY don't have sailors, they cannot sail
If THEY don't have jailers, they cannot jail
If THEY have no soldiers, we'll NOT HAVE WAR
And civilization can try it once more.

title: Off Shore Drilling
lyrics:
Drilling off shore to get more oil, gnarl y gooDrilling off shore to get more oil, gnarl y goo
Drill baby drill to get more oil, B P and Shell will,
Get the spoil, Ick y stink y gnarl y goo

Old Ronny Reagan had his flaws, gnarl y goo
Old Ronny Reagan had his flaws, gnarl y goo
Old Ronny Reagan flawed hard core, Still he banned drilling
Off the shore, Ick y stink y gnarl y goo

Little George Bush who had no clue, gnarl y goo
Little George Bush who had no clue, gnarl y goo
Little George Bush, God help the boob, He flushed that ban right
Down the tube, Ick y stink y gnarl y goo

Barack Obama lost his way, gnarl y goo
Barack Obama lost his way, gnarl y goo
Barack Obama lost his way, never too late to
Change your game, Ick y stink y gnarl y goo

Carbon we belch, in heat we squelch, gnarl y goo
Carbon we belch, in heat we squelch, gnarl y goo
Polar ice caps will surely melt, Oceans will flood the
Coastal plains, Ick y stink y gnarl y goo.

title: Enjoy Yourself
lyrics:
Enjoy yourself, it's later than you thinkWe activists, will wind up in the clink
The Feds close in, as quickly as a wink
Enjoy yourself, deploy yourself,
It's later than you think!

You demonstrate, for years and years
With signs and shouts to show
You hate us being in Iraq
To steal their oil, we know.
Some day they say, there will be peace
Our government will rule
Resistance fighters will be dead
Democracy is cruel.

Chorus (Enjoy yourself…..)
tune: Enjoy Yourself, It's Later Than You Think

the lies that led us into war,

are off of the charts!

their stories are phoney!

title: Bailout!
lyrics:
From the <strong>fall <strong>of Lehman Brothers to the fall of AIG,We will <strong>bail <strong>out all investment firms and insurance companies.
For we <strong>bank<strong>rolled lots of S&Ls, but screwed Enron employees,
So <strong>if<strong> your house has been foreclosed, don't ask for our help, please.

If Ka<strong>trin<strong>a blew your house away when you lived in New Orleans,
And we<strong> gave<strong> you toxic mobile homes and we washed away your dreams,
Don't come <strong>whining<strong> to the White House now, or to the Capitol Dome.
We'll de<strong>reg<strong>ulate the USA 'til the rich own all the homes.

If <strong>you<strong> get sick, don’t come to us, 'cause we don’t give a damn.
And your <strong>pensions<strong> tanked? That really stank, but don’t look to Uncle Sam.
We are <strong>giving<strong> all your taxes now to the fat cats, they’re our friends.
And there’s <strong>nothing <strong>you can do at all; corporate welfare never ends.

Oh, we’re <strong>laughing<strong> all along the way to the bank that we now own!
‘Cause there’s <strong>no <strong>more regulation on those mortgages and loans.
Oh, we <strong>have<strong> no funds to fix our schools, or our roads or libraries.
Seven <strong>hundred <strong>billion down the drain while we bring you to your knees.

Well, Mc<strong>Cain<strong> has six or seven homes ’though he isn’t really sure;
And <strong>Sarah’s <strong>wardrobe cost more than my house plus yours and yours.
Oh, de<strong>reg<strong>ulation’s quite a scheme, and there’s nothing you can do....
Unless we <strong>get <strong>out there and use our votes to turn all those red states blue!

title: Santa Doesn't Like War Toys
lyrics:
Oh, you better watch outYou better not buy
Toys for the kids
That are violent, Oh fie!
Santa Claus is coming to town.

Santa says no to submachine guns
Legos and games are much more fun
Santa Claus is coming to town

The reindeer all cooperate
As they fly on their way
We can teach our kids the same
In the ways they learn to play

Oh, you better watch out
You'd better not buy
Toys for the kids
That are violent, oh fie!
Santa Claus is coming to town.
tune: Santa Claus is Coming to Town

title: Gotta Change the System
lyrics:
So.......th’ system didn’t work so well...It didn’t catch the crooks....
Even looked the other way for years
While Madoff cooked the books!
Madoff....made off with 50 billion
In his Ponzi schemes.....
We gotta change the system
To foil crooked dreams.

Th’ economy and the markets
Are not cast in stone....
Not geologic crystals...
Unchanging.....stand-alone....
They’re systems that were designed by men
That need an overhaul....
We can create another one....while
This one goes.....over th’ waterfall!

2157

title: See How the Money Flies
lyrics:
Ding-Dong....see how money flies....In all its many guises....
It’s only paper anyway...
In all its many sizes...
Does anybody know what gives
With hedge funds and derivatives?
Swaps ‘n Ponzis......and financial flips?
Seems head honchos don’t know either!

Ding-Dong....pie-in-the-sky....
Watch out for money movers.....
They’ll distract your attention every time...
And then your dough gets Hoovered!
It’s made of debt....our money system....
The real McCoy seems to have missed ‘em....
And those stocks you lost, hope you kissed ‘em....
.......Goodbye.....
‘Cause.......they won’t come back either!

2149

title: Cleaning Out the Lies
lyrics:
[I wrote this song to be sung on the day before Obama took office, when anti-Bush demonstrators were staging a “smudging” ceremony at the White House.]
We <strong>sweep <strong>and sanitize
To <strong>clean <strong>out Bush’s lies!
He <strong>thought <strong>that he could fool us
But we’re <strong>Grannies <strong>old and wise.

We’ll <strong>sing <strong>it loud and clear:
Bush <strong>played <strong>upon our fears.
He <strong>lied <strong>so we’d support a war
That’s been <strong>draggin’ <strong>on for years.

We’ll <strong>sweep <strong>until we’re clean
Of <strong>right<strong>-wing corporate schemes.
They <strong>made <strong>a profit on the war...
That <strong>really <strong>is obscene!

Eight <strong>years <strong>was far too long.
It’s <strong>jail <strong>where Bush belongs!
And <strong>when <strong>the people find their voice
He’ll <strong>sing <strong>a different song!

Good <strong>riddance<strong>, Mr. Bush!
We’ll <strong>help <strong>you with a push
Right <strong>through <strong>the White House door.
Don’t let it <strong>hit <strong>you in the tush!

So <strong>off<strong> to work we go!
Give <strong>Bush <strong>the big heave-ho!
We’ll <strong>clean <strong>out all his bullshit (and)
Give <strong>change <strong>a chance to grow!

title: Sit-in by the Impeachment Tree
lyrics:
Congress shouldn't impeach George BushUnless they dump Cheney too.
Cheney is Number Two:
Number Two means doo-doo.
Doo, doo, doo!
George and Dick played dirty tricks
To send our troops to war.
We want our troops back home.

Sit-in by the impeachment tree,
Subpoenas will fall like fruit
From trees rotten to the root.
Give George and Dick the boot!
Scoot! Scoot! Scoot!
We can pressure them to resign.
We won't have to prosecute
To bring our troops back home.

Now that you've heard,
Go mail your words
Tell Congress “Don’t delay:
Clean the Bush White House
Of every louse
Who sent our troops away!
Hey!

Till we hold hearings on George and Dick
We won't get our troops back home,
Let them come marching home.
Let them come marching home!
tune: Don't Sit Under The Apple Tree

title: Wasteful Military Spending
lyrics:
Death unending!  Military spending!What a waste!  What a waste!
Reinstate some sanity,
Spend it for humanity,
Invest in jobs! Invest in peace!

Let’s build schools instead of bombs,
Let’s feed the poor!  Let’s feed the poor!
Don’t spend all our taxes
To fight the “evil axis.”
Invest in schools!  Invest in peace!

Missile shields and nuclear weapons --
What a waste! What a waste!
While we’re busy making war
We’re neglecting our own poor!
Invest in health!  Invest in peace!

“Richest nation in the world!”
What a joke!  Don’t make me choke!
Our kids are being left behind!
How can people be so blind?
Invest in kids! Invest in peace!

title: Single Payer Blues
lyrics:
I went to the insurance company.They turned my claim down there,
Said it wasn’t covered,
And they really didn’t care.

O, I went to see my doctor,
And he said he couldn’t spend
Fifteen minutes with me!
Such things have got to end.

Oh, my pills they cost me plenty,
And I know I’m going broke.
This new fangled health care,
Truly is no joke.

So, send Congress a message:
Pass single payer now!
We all really need it—
It’s good for us, AND HOW.

title: That's What It's All About
lyrics:
We put our right hand in as we work for wars to cease,We raise our right arm high as we join the march for peace.
We raise our voices loudly as we Grannies gather 'round,
That's what it's all about!

We put our left hand in for the children everywhere,
We raise our left arm high, we've got lots of love to share.
We raise our voices proudly as we Grannies gather 'round,
That's what it's all about!

We put our right leg in as we join both young and old,
We kick our right leg high as our raging's growing bold.
Each day our voice gets louder as we Grannies gather 'round,
That's what it's all about!

We put our left leg in, now let's kick the bullies out!
We put our left foot down, 'cause we Grannies have such clout!
We'll change the world together as we Grannies gather 'round,
That's what it's all about!

We put our whole self in 'cause our whole self's what it takes,
We put our whole self in 'cause we all know the stakes.
We will sing together until peace and justice win,
That's what it's all about!
songwriter: Sharon Ann Wurmann and Vicki Lewin Ryder

title: This Old Gray Granny
lyrics:
This OLD gray Granny ain’t what she used to be,HAD a hysterectomy,  NEEDS a colonoscopy,
But SHE can’t afford to PAY for her care and so
I GUESS we’ll have to shoot her now.

‘Cause WHERE is she supposed to go
WHEN she doesn’t have the dough?
She DARE not get sick without health insurance so
I GUESS we’ll have to shoot her now.

This OLD gray Granny SPLITS all her pills in half.
The DRUG companies just laugh; Their PROFITS are off the graph.
But GRANNY can’t afford to PAY for her pills and so
I GUESS we’ll have to shoot her now.

‘Cause WHAT is she supposed to do
When MONEY for the rent is due?
She CAN’T buy pills and GROCERIES, too, and so
I GUESS we’ll have to shoot her now.

This OLD gray Granny NOW needs a test or two.
Her BOOB has a lump, it’s true. But WHAT is she supposed to do?
She CAN’T pay the bill so she’ll JUST have to muddle through.
I GUESS we’ll have to shoot her now.

Well, GRANNY’s old but she is wise.
She KNOWS we have to organize,
So CALL up your rep and SENators, too.
We want SINGLE payer HEALTH care for us all!

Just GIVE your Representatives a call,
And TELL ‘em we need health care, one and all!
Then OLD gray granny WON’T have to worry
‘Cause we won't have to SHOOT her after all!

title: What Do We Want from Obama?
lyrics:
<strong>What<strong> do we want from Obama,The <strong>man<strong> we'd like to love?

To <strong>him<strong> on change-dot-gov:

We<strong> want<strong> our troops brought home real quick
And <strong>all <strong>the wars to cease,
’Cause <strong>bombs <strong>and occupation
Are<strong> not<strong> the way to peace.

We want <strong>fair <strong>and equal treatment
For <strong>folks<strong> both straight and gay,
And an <strong>end<strong> to corporate bailouts...
<strong>Those <strong>with the most should pay!

We want<strong> single<strong>-payer health care,
Or<strong> else<strong> you’ll hear us rage,
And <strong>jobs <strong>that stay right here at home
And <strong>pay<strong> a living wage.

Re<strong>store<strong> our civil liberties!
We <strong>don’t <strong>know where they went...
And an <strong>end<strong> to the label of terrorist
For <strong>those<strong> who dare dissent.

<strong>Stop<strong> the use of torture.
<strong>Justice<strong> must prevail!
But <strong>here’s<strong> the thing that we want most –
<strong>Cheney<strong> and Bush in jail!

<strong>That’s<strong> what we want from Obama,
It <strong>must<strong> be our demand!
To <strong>set <strong>our country right again,
Put <strong>Cheney<strong> and Bush in the can!

title: The Grannies' Lament
lyrics:
Oh, pity the poor Raging Grannies, how awful!Now that Election Day’s over and gone....

We’ve raged about war in Iraq and the bailouts,

We’ll house all the homeless and feed all the hungry,
We’ll put folks to work and we’ll end all the wars.
We’ll have fair elections and make living wages....
These are the changes we’ve been raging for.

If we’re feeling hopeful how can we be raging?
Our despair has been lifted – our shoulders are light.
It’s time now to do what Obama’s requested:
To roll up our sleeves and start setting things right.

CHORUS

So we all can tell him what he should now do.

Put George Bush in jail and then Dick Cheney, too!

CHORUS

title: Change? What Change? (A Granny Rap)
lyrics:
<em>[We know that brown's the new black<em> is a hip way of saying that we're up on the trends of the day. If you have a concern that this  line might possibly be misinterpreted as referring to race, then by all means change it....)
We Grannies are hip to the trends of the day
And we know that brown’s the new black;
We know there’s a change in our government
And George is now Barack!

We heard he’ll fix our economy
With a roarin’ stimulus,
Create new jobs and cure our ills...
But here’s what troubles us:

Obama’s still talkin’ ‘bout goin’ to war;
Afghanistan’s the new Iraq.
But wherever our armies are fixin’ to go,
We Grannies say “Bring ‘em back!”

We thought that things would change a bit
With Obama in command,
And it makes us rage that he’s fixin’ to send
Our troops to Afghanistan.

Sending our troops to Afghanistan
Is a crazy thing to do.
They routed the Brits and the Soviets
And they’ll kick our butts there, too!

How many more of our good kids
Will die on foreign shores?
Obama or Bush, we see no change
When it comes to these God-damned wars!

How many civilians of other lands
Will die for imperial aims?
How many lives will we snuff out
For greedy corporate gain?

Afghanistan or Pakistan...
War is so yesterday!
We voted for change, not more of the same.
So end all wars today!

title: Foreclosure
lyrics:
FORECLOSURE
We <strong>once<strong> had a home

Taken away, their homes won’t be taken away.

And CONgress will hear what they say.

Now<strong> most<strong> of us know
When the <strong>rich <strong>run the show

So we’d <strong>all <strong>better care
Or we ALL will be singing someday:

REFRAIN
Taken away! Our homes have been taken away.

That we didn’t vote for,
But our homes have been taken away.

) Tune: Is There Anybody Here Who Loves My Jesus? (Old gospel)

title: Montreal Massacre Names
lyrics:
This refers to the Montreal Massacre in 1989, a very important women's event in Canada. I think its important to try and remember victim's names, if at all possible, in situations like this. If you group them and rhyme them, it is easier. And to try hard not to mention or remember the name of their killer. This song is especially important because they were killed, only because they were women, he said. If you sing it over a few times, you will actually be able to remember all 14 names - And forget the fully sane, abuser who ended their lives.
While you sing this song, which is ONLY their names, you hear Brahm’s words underneath.  And you are able to wish them to sleep in peace while calling angels to help them sleep without fear:

Anne-Marie Lemay, Maud Hav-iernick, Gen-e-vieve Ber-ger-on

Nath-alie Croteau, Sonia Pel-let-ier (Pelchay) , Hel-ene- Col-gan

Maryse La-gan-iere, Maryse Le-Clair

Barbara Klu-cz-nick, Barbara Dai-gn-eault

(Here repeat tune from Maryse ) Annie Tur-cotte, Anne Marie Edward

Annie St Arneault, Mich-ele, Ri-chard (Reeshaw)

If you sing it over a few times, you will actually be able to remember all 14 names

And forget the fully sane, abuser who ended their lives.

You’ve got the whole yard

Life patenting regimes

Just some of their grand schemes

A family farm is

Just feathered & tarred

Public plant breeding

Could still save the day

No private interests

Should get in the way

So if you write a little letter

Or 2 or 3 or 4 (Or even MORE)

For farmers

To help save their seed!

title: Seed Need
lyrics:
Who’d want to be a farmer

With terminator seed?

Single season seed?

Plain old rental seed?

Removing merit testing

That’s more corporate greed

We’re asking you to

SAVE OUR SEED

The Seed’s Act, we need it

Custom and tradition

Both are needed

If we’re going to save our seed!
tune: Hanging On The Old Barbed Wire

title: Seed Business
lyrics:
There’s NO business like seed business, like no business I know
Such a little miracle a seed is
Kernels can contain a 10 ft stalk
Once they could be saved & planted over
Traded for clover

But now they’re locked
Into bad business in seed business
Monsanto, Bayer & Dow
But there’s the farmer’s union

Which will NEVER fold
Nor leave the farmer out in the cold
Nor deregulate the system for a sack of gold
Let’s get into their show

And make this show a GO!

title: Farmers Rights
lyrics:
Let us give foreverThe right to save their seeds
To our nation’s farmers
Wherever they may be
Right to reuse and select
Right to sell & trade
No changes to tradition
That a farmer’s hard work made

CHORUS
Enshrine their rights forever
In-alien-able
No restricting seed use
Free & plentiful

V2) Age-old farmer’s custom
Was to save their seeds
Corporate change is seeking
To criminalize the deed
Impeding innovation
Taking over all
Already farmer’s numbers
Are growing very small

CHORUS

title: 2nd Sit In At Recruiting Station
lyrics:
(From Holiday Inn, Christmas Movie, Bing & Kaye)
We’ll follow our conscience
Whatever it tells us to
Even if we must say
‘Let me enlist today.’
We’re loving our grandkids
Far far too much to stay
Back on our rocking chairs, out of the way!
Because we love them
We love them
The YOUNG men and women swallowed by this war
And the grans are here to answer duty’s call
We won’t let this war make soldiers of them all!

title: PlayStation: War Is Not a Videogame
lyrics:
He was just a normal, ordinary high school boy.His Game Boy and his X-Box games gave him great joy.
He was the best guy at those games.
But it was only for fun
Till the recruiters came.
He joined the Army then,
And now his claim to fame
Is his high score killing human beings without getting blamed.

She was just a normal, ordinary high school girl.
Her X-Box and her Game Boy really rocked her world.
She was the best girl at those games.
But it was only for fun
Till the recruiters came.
She joined the Army then.
They taught her better aim,
But when civilians died her guilt could not be restrained.

Bang, boom, deedlee-ee beep
Recruiting stations these days
Use simulations
Where kids sit in tanks and blow up bad guys
From foreign nations.
Recruiters tell the kids
War’s like a videogame,
But after real-life battles kids are never the same.

Most kids feel immortal and think they can’t die.
They can’t imagine injuries might wreck their lives,
Or that post-traumatic stress
Can shake up their brains,
And leave them depressed.
Recruiting stations wear
PlayStation camouflage.
Don’t let them draw our kids in with a high-tech mirage.

title: Faith-Based Initiatives
lyrics:
Drop those faith-based initiatives,Stop those faith-based divisions,
Don’t make faith-based decisions,
It’s unconstitutional.

End that church-state integration!
We want church-state separation!
It was good enough for Jefferson,
It’s good enough for us.

They hang crosses in the halls,
Ten Commandments on the walls,
Christmas wreaths in bathroom stalls,
It’s unconstitutional.

They will not hire gay people
Under minarets and steeples,
Denouncing them as evil,
It’s unconstitutional.

They fund Muslims, Jews, and Christians,
Every sect and cult and schism,
They don’t fund atheism:
That’s unconstitutional.

We need secularization
To have justice in our nation.
Give us church-state separation,
And we’ll all have liberty.

February 22, 2009, with assistance from Lindsay Audin

What are we fightin’ for?

Join us, all good women and men,

The Constitution’s been under attack

If our troops don’t lay down their guns,

 CHORUS IRAQ:

What are we fightin’ for?

First it was WMD,

Now it’s “stability.”

And it’s five, six, seven,

Force the Congress to open their eyes

 CHORUS AFGHANISTAN:

What are we fightin’ for?

Let’s leave Afganistan.

And it’s five, six, seven,

CHORUS AFGHANISTAN:

title: Don’t Let a Smile Be Your Umbrella
lyrics:
Don’t let a smile be your umbrellaOn a sunny, sunny day.
Because you may get melanoma
Till the ozone layer’s okay.
A parasol is your best line of defense.
Though we stopped using hairspray,
The danger’s immense.
Pour on your sunscreen, wear a broad hat
On a sunny, sunny day.

Don’t breathe in “fresh air”
’Cause there is none
On a breezy, breezy day.
Don’t let your lungs fill with pollutants
Or with asthma you will pay.
Emissions from big cars can make us all choke.
And coal that’s “clean” is just a myth, it’s mirrors and smoke.
So stay indoors and use air filters
On a breezy, breezy day.

Go out and buy a strong umbrella
For a hurricane-y day.
While climate change keeps changing weather,
Greenhouse gases have their way.
While the USA hid NASA’s reports,
Wind-surfing through tsunamis was the world’s hottest sport.
Let’s stop our polar caps from melting
On these sunny, sunny days.

[OPTIONAL TAG:
It’s inconvenient--
Shrink carbon footprints right away!]

June 6, 2008, revised using scientific advisories from Lindsay Audin, 03109
tune: Let a Smile Be Your Umbrella

title: Waterboarding
lyrics:
When the water’s freezing cold,Waterboarding,
Chills the body and scars the soul,
Waterboarding.

When you struggle to  inhale,
Waterboarding,
Your tormentors might prevail.
Waterboarding.

On TV, instead of lies,
Waterboarding,
Cheney said he authorized
Waterboarding.

Obama, it is time,
Waterboarding,
To set up tri-als for war crimes,
Like Waterboarding.

March 2008, updated December 22, 2008

title: Indian Point Must Go!
lyrics:
<strong>Indian Point Must Go!<strong>
<strong>Tune: My Bonnie Lies Over the Ocean <strong>

<strong>We went to an NRC meeting.
What we sang they did not want to hear:
Don't dare to extend this plant's license
By even as much as one year.<strong>

<strong>Yes, Entergy promises safety,
But sometimes their sirens don't blow,
And tritium leaks are polluting
So Indian Point has to go!<strong>

<strong>CHORUS:
No nukes,
No nukes,
Indian Point has to go
Right now!
No nukes,
No nukes,
Yes Indian Point has to go!<strong>

<strong>This plant is unsafe for employees
But we don't want anyone fired.
Retrain them for alternate energy
And make sure that they get rehired.<strong>

<strong>This plant is a terrorist target.
Why not use much less toxic fuels?
Convert to a natural gas plant!
Secure those nuclear waste pools.<strong>

<strong>REPEAT CHORUS:
<strong>
<strong>The route for our evacuation
Won't work! We can't get out of town!
What good are clogged roads for escapin'
If Indian Point does melt down? <strong>

<strong>Our State DEC made a ruling
Hot water kills too many fish.
If Entergy can't build new coolers,
Relicensing plans should be squished. <strong>

<strong>SPOKEN: Then we Grannies will get our wish!<strong>

<strong>VARIATION ON CHORUS<strong>

<strong>Shut it down!
Shut it down!
Indian Point is unsafe, we know!
Shut it down!
Shut it down!
Indian Point has to go!<strong>

This song was inspired by “Cleanup Hanford” by Kay Thode of the Seattle Raging Grannies. The subject of the verse about retraining employees was added at suggestion of Pete Seeger when he sang an earlier version of this song with three Raging Grannies gaggles at a festival in 2007. Some of us sang another version to a meeting of the NRC (Nuclear Regulatory Commission) and the Entergy Corporation, owner of the Indian Point nuclear power plant in New York.
tune: My Bonnie Lies Over the Ocean

title: Where Does Taxpayer Money Go? (NYC)
lyrics:
Where does taxpayer money go?Where does taxpayer money go?
Where does taxpayer money go?
The working class should have a say.

The Governor makes us feel like weak beseechers
Because we want schools to have lots of good teachers.
Our hospital buildings should have the best features.
Give us Fair Share Tax Reform!

Where does taxpayer money go?
Where does taxpayer money go?
Where does taxpayer money go?
Give us Fair Share Tax Reform.

The workers pay taxes but so should their bosses.
If the rich paid their share, we would cut all our losses.
Don’t dump our good programs, please hold back your horses!
Give us Fair Share Tax Reform.

Adapted from Where Does Taxpayer Money Go? by Jan Harwood, Santa Cruz Raging Grannies, for a rally held
March 5, 2009
tune: Oh Dear! What Can the Matter Be

title: Anti-Nuke Environmentalists
lyrics:
Call us anti-nuke environmentalists.We are anti-nuke environmentalists.
We protect our air and water,
You can't lead us to the slaughter,
'Cause we're anti-nuke environmentalists.

We know tons of facts regarding nuclear waste.
When it leaks into our water there's no taste,
But it's poison all the same.
Entergy is who we blame,
So we're anti-nuke environmentalists.

If you get your self relicensed and still run,
There'll be lots more dead fish lying in the sun,
More strontium in our babies' teeth,
More leaks that millions hate to breathe,
With no evacuation they can't run.

There are twenty tons of new waste every year,
All created by the Indian Point plant here.
You can swear on our own Bibles
That it's “safe, secure, and vital,”
But we're sure not gonna swallow what we hear.

When we think about Chernobyl, we have qualms.
You're a target for those terrorists with bombs.
There's no anti-nuke insurance,
Which means there's no assurance
That we will not all be blown to Kingdom Come.

Bring us solar, bring us hydro, bring us wind.
Bring us energy from sources that won't end.
Before we could trust uranium,
We'd need holes in our cranium.
Call us anti-nuke environmentalists.

February 10, 2009: Lyrics adapted from “Radical Environmentalist”by Kay Thode of the Seattle Raging Grannies

Note: Entergy runs the Indian Point nuclear power plant, which it is trying to get re-licensed for 20 more years. Safe, secure, and vital” is the plant's slogan.

title: Taking a Chance on Nukes
lyrics:
Here we go again,We hear the sirens blow again,
There’s an eerie glow again,
Taking a chance on nukes.

White House policy
Says all our electricity
Needs nuclear energy,
Making their choice for nukes.

They hand out pills for our thyroids
And say we won’t die.
But unless we are androids
Our other glands will fry.

Moth’r Earth, we love her here.
Hijackers flew above her here.
We want no duck-and-cover here,
Taking a chance on nukes.

We know climate change will be global,
From old fossil fuels.
But another Chernobyl
Could warp all our gene pools.

Can you stand this strife?
It feels like walking on a knife.
Let kids live more than half a life!
Don’t take a chance on nukes.

April 22, 2008

lyrics:

Accomplished that.

Upon their chests
We are grans

Our experience says
No war for us
Lay down those guns
Start to discuss.

title: Canada Was For Peace
lyrics:
You can find someone singing this on Youtube. Vancouver sang it to another tune as well and I don't know what that was. You can always call me up and I can sing it to you over the phone. This original tune is easy & catchy.
Up in Canada
You could flee the war
Peace was practiced everywhere
They said, in Canada.
In the USA,
When the war was on,
We could start again
Up in Canada.
So, many left their homes,
I was one who came
Seeking for the peace that lived,
Up in Canada

Now in Canada,
Listening to the States,
Missing from this government
Is any talk of peace.
In the USA,
Wartalk going on,
Drafting of their young,
I've heard it all before.
But where is hope to be?
If Canada spurns peace?
Let's go back to where peace lived,
Once here, in Canada.

title: Your Tax Dollars (2009)
lyrics:
Oh <strong>where,<strong> oh where, have your tax dollars gone?They’re<strong> funding<strong> the war in Iraq!
They spend <strong>taxes <strong>on war
But they <strong>don’t<strong> help the poor.

lyrics:

title: Violence
lyrics:
This is the most nonviolent tune I have ever heard. We sing it with men. When it comes to 'Women's stories,' we split off. They sing 'Men's stories,' at the same time, then go on to say, 'men' while we say, 'women,' and at the end it's 'change by all.' Because just as men need to be called to account for their attitudes towards violence, in the opposite way, so do women - stronger & braver for starters. (Rose DeShaw)
Violence, violence

Eats away at all of us

Violence has faces, scary spaces, Accomplices

Violence, violence

Movies, tv, books & jokes

Misogyny a regular host

Fear the most, innocence lost

Violence, violence needs women’s stories,

Women  strong

Women’s voices, women’s songs

And CHANGE by all!

Peterborough Gaggle

lyrics:

These deaths

 3)  Gee, we are here to enlist in the army

title: Legal's Gone Astray
lyrics:
These two transported illegal border crossers who were dying in the sun and were arressted and charged. While it is a local issue about the Mexican border, the larger issue is human rights. This song can be adapted and updated for new circumstance. It's a great old civil war tune.
Shanti Sellz and Daniel Strauss
Face prison time it’s true
For transporting the dying
What if it had been me or you?
If it’s legal to just let men die
Then legal’s gone astray
And that’s why we’re marching here today
CHORUS
No deaths, no more
Not due to silly laws
No deaths, no more
We vow no more because
A human is a human
Makes no difference who they be
We’re all in the same family!

title: Dandelions Are GOOD For You
lyrics:
We made a dandelion costume out of yellow & green garbage bags & had a can of raid chase it around during the two last verses. You can get young people to do this.
Dandelion flowers are a little sign of hope
Whoever poisons them on lawns would have to be a dope.
Or else someone who doesn’t know
what dandelions do
For our insides and our outsides and for our whole world too.
2) When dandelions are all grown
Their puffy heads turn grey
And with  my breath I blow their fluff
and watch it float away.
And in the spring it comes again
For us to pick and eat
A yellow dandelion lawn
Is Mother Nature’s treat
3) If every lawn grew dandelions
Our air would be so sweet
Our tummies would be happy
They would tickle all our feet.
Every flower stands on guard
To help us day by day
I’ll have a dandelion lawn
Where you can come and Play!
THEN WE ADD;
tune: Jeremiah was a Bullfrog
Dandelion lawns now
For the boys & girls now
No more poisons in the deep blue sea
Lawns for you and me!
 Repeat 3 times, clap and form conga line
then chant  We love dandelions!

title: Write Your Congressperson!
lyrics:
Darling, I am growing sickerAnd I know we're out of gold
But our government can fix this
If we make sure that they're told

*Single user pay's the answer
Write your congressperson dear
With the proper health insurance
I'll be better, never fear.

title: Who Needs Health Care Plan?
lyrics:
North of the borderIn New Mexico
That's where we need a break
For goodness sake
On health care rates
And we sure don't wonder
We know what we need
*Single user pay plan
Is welcome indeed!
I - yi-yi-yi (point to self)
And You, you you! (point to audience)

title: New Health Care System
lyrics:
One little, two little, three little dollars
Gotta squeeze 'em until they holler
Where's the best place to put that money?
Make a new health care system!

One little, two little, three little sick ones
Spreading their germs with no dough to fix 'em
We've gotta get us a brand new system
Have we got a plan for you!

*Single user pay, that's the ticket
It will work but we all gotta pick it
Anything else, tell them where to stick it!
Single user pay now!

title: Refugee's Song
lyrics:
We who havehome and friends
life so full and free
pause to look
at the plight
of the refugees
Where there's food
shelter too
this must be their home
life goes on
day by day
when will peacetime come?
things they once
held most dear
vanished now it seems
loved ones gone
life is hard
yet they still have dreams
Thanks to help from friends
Hope that never ends
tune: Largo' from Dvorak's New World Symphony or Going Home

title: What Granny's Can Expect
lyrics:
It may-y be hard for you to take a stand
Even though you see
what's happening in our world
But if you step out strong
They'll be others come along
Till one by one our voices can be heard
2) They may-y laugh
Call yu names to shut you up
They might be trouble
Somewhere on the way
But if you come in peace
Understanding will increase
Till bit by bit there comes
A better day
3) So let us go
Talk to others when we can
About the things around us
going down
And if you do your share
You'll find that folks still care
Till day by day we've turned
This world around.

title: [Bad Politican Name here] The Knife
lyrics:
Brackets indicate where anothe rpoliticans name might be added
Oh our welfare...now is workfare
Single mothers...cry and hide
It's so hard for...them to work dear
With their toddlers...at their side
2) when the cheque comes...it's been cut, dear
and the food bank...fights for life
Someone's sneaking...round the corner
Is that someone...[Mike] the Knife?
3) Education...is a right, dear,
Until [Harris]...shows his might
Cuts the contract...forces teachers
To leave classrooms...go on strike
4) What to do now...is it too late?
People suffering...in despair
Let's take action...Let's start marching
The Knife, [Mike} ...BEWARE!

title: Politicans and Raging Grannies
lyrics:
Politics and Raging GranniesSymbiotically
Go together, quite non-partisan
Voluntarily
Like those little birds on hippos
Looking for the fleas
If we elect 'em
We'll inspect 'em
Very publically!
2) Politicans - Raging Grannies
What a useful fit
When they stray from what they promise
We see every bit
Then we put these flaws to music
(Though it often makes our hearts sick)
We called it right on [Stockwell Day]
We know what makes [him] tick!
3) If you wanna run for office
(even some grannies do)
Then be prepared to keep your promises
Watch just what you do
Make a difference to our country
Sacrificing wealth and bounty
And check back often with the folks
Who have elected you.

title: Women Of The World Be Strong
lyrics:
step by step and step by stepwomen of the world be strong
day by day and day by day
wipe the blood and tears away
save our sisters from their terrors
cut the chains that warp and bind
open doors till we are free
leave oppression far behind

2) step by step and step by step
women of the world be strong
do not wait, we must go on
though the road be dark and long
come with us to touch the bright sky
as our own free voices ring
with the breath of inspiration
flying forward on the wind

3) step by step and step by step
we shall go triumphantly
towards a new humanity
women of the world are we

title: Water System Failure
lyrics:
In Walkerton beside the riverWaters flowed clean
Till farming turned to agri-business
E-coli changed the scene
System cutbacks failed the people
Many of them died
(sarcastically) How could this happen
In our [province]
With [Mike Harris] on our side?

title: This Park is MY Park
lyrics:
This park is your parkThis park is my park
The wind was blowing
The man was spraying
We play and sit here
We bring our kids here
This park was made for you and me.
Look at Toronto
Forget Monsanto
Can we walk barefoot?
Where 2-4-D's put?
Is clover noxious?
Are WE obnoxious? (sweet smile)
This park was made for you and me.
this Park is my park
this park is your park
From spring till winter
In light and in dark
We play and sit here
We bring our kids here
This park was made for you and me
WE HOPE!!

title: God Help America
lyrics:
God Help America(Tune: “God Bless America”)

God help America
We need you BAD!
Cause our leaders are cheaters
And they’re making the world really mad.

Climbing Mountains, crossing oceans
And invading foreign soil...
God help America
No blood for oil,
God forgive America
No blood for oil!

title: Child of War
lyrics:
What child is this we lay to rest,his body broken and bleeding,
who lost his life to war and strife,
while the world went on unheeding?

What child is this who cannot rest,
hungry, thirsty, no one aiding,
with parents lost, she pays the cost
And for her the light is fading.

This, this is a child of war
We’ve lost so many, please - no more!
Haste, haste, to end these wars
And to save all humanity’s children.

What kind of folk can ignore their plight
And not feel for the little ones crying?
Living with pain, calling out in vain.
Can we keep ignoring the dying?

Man’s greed has caused the fights
Though they say their cause is just and right...
Fools, fools, will they ever care
That they’re killing humanity’s children?

title: Anti-Recruiter Song
lyrics:
ANTI-RECRUITER SONG
Recruiters are really in a bind,
new recruits they simply cannot find.
Their quotas can’t be met
and they’re getting real upset
cause they talk a lot but get nothing signed.

So recruiters troll in the high schools
and lie and break their own codes and rules,
cause enlistments rates are down
but,  recruiters, (pause) look around,
it doesn’t work cause these kids are not fools!

But if you’re between 16 and 24,
they could come knocking at your door,
cause they’ve got a database
that lets them easily trace
kids to fight and die for these stupid wars

But give it up, recruiters, they won’t go
cause they’re all smart enough to know
that the chance of coming back
from Afghanistan or Iraq
unmaimed and alive is way too low!

You need to know that we don’t want this war,
cause they’ve  lied to us about what it’s for,
so we’ll protest and wave our signs
your jobs to undermine
so our kids won’t lose their lives anymore!
tune: If You're Happy and You Know It

title: Soldier Boy
lyrics:
SOLDIER BOY
Come, they told him,  Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
The war drums call for you,  Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
Your country has need of you  Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
It’s the right thing to do
Pah rum-pum-pum-pum, rum pum-pum-pum, rum pum-pum-pum
All...you need to do   Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
Is shoulder this gun.

Come, they told him.  Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
We’ll pay you cash  up front   Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
The enemy to confront   Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
Come join our glorious hunt
Pah rum-pum-pum-pum, rum pum-pum-pum, rum pum-pum-pum
Be...one of the few   Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
Who never will run.

So, he fought for them  Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
So many years of war   Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
Anguished and heartsore   Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
What was he fighting for?
Pah rum-pum-pum-pum, rum pum-pum-pum, rum pum-pum-pum
They...taught him new skills  Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
He learned how to kill.

He heard them talk of peace   Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
While he saw atrocities   Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
Crimes against humanity   Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
Torture with immunity
Pah rum-pum-pum-pum, rum pum-pum-pum, rum pum-pum-pum
He watched so many die  Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
And learned not to cry.

No more, he told them   Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
Looking out from dead eyes   Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
He asked them why they lied  Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
Challenged them to deny
Pah rum-pum-pum-pum, rum pum-pum-pum, rum pum-pum-pum
He turned his back on them   Pah rum-pum-pum-pum
And threw down his gun...
Rum pum pum pum....
Rum...pum...pum...pum......

title: PTSD
lyrics:
Note - this song emphasizes the horrors of war. It also describes the feelings of despair in a true story of one individual at the beginning of his personal struggle with PTSD. We know now that each experience of PTSD is unique and that all can be successfully overcome with patience and courage, yes, even memories such as this.]
Inspired by <em>A Deserter’s Tale<em>, by Joshua Key

Still so young, I’m just 18,
But I suffer night and day
My illness is PTSD
Will it ever go away

A country boy with chances slim
For college and career
Recruiters came to school to pour
Sweet poison in my ears

Your tuition, paid in full
If you sign right here
The army will take care of you
You’ll never go to war

To my dismay I was deployed
To the war zone of Iraq
Death, destruction everywhere,
It could not be more bleak

Told to raid a house each night
The terrorists to catch
But in the houses all we found
Were kids, their moms and dads

The men, we took away with us
No reason did we give
And left the women wond’ring if
They’d e’er be back alive

One time a baby cried and cried
Because I’d hurt her mom
I could not take the guilt and shame
And so discharged my gun

And then that baby’s crying stopped
It stopped forevermore
It haunts me now with all the rest
Of my Iraqi war.

title: Cindy's Song
lyrics:
Hey Mr. President, why won’t you talk to me,
I’ve been waiting and I’m not planning to be leavin’.
Hey Mr. President, why won’t you talk to me,
I’ll be here in the morning still a-grievin’. .

Though my son’s now gone from me and never can return,
his memory still burns,
thoughts of needless death turn and spin while I stand waiting.
Your unconcern amazes me, I need to hear you say
that there was no other way,
and that this war’s about something more than simply hating

Hey Mr. President, why won’t you talk to me,
I’m still waitin’ and there is no place I’m goin’ to.
Hey Mr. President, why won’t you talk to me,
my empty heart is grievin’ and I’m waitin’ for you.

Tell me why you believe our sons and daughters have to die...
you shake your head and sigh, but I’ve heard too many lies,
your government denies that our troops die only for oil and power,
I’m tired of the excuses, there were no WMDs,
and spreadin’ democracy just doesn’t seem to me
to have anything to do with nine-eleven and the Towers.

Hey Mr. President, why won’t you talk to me,
I’m still waiting and not planning to be leavin’.
Hey Mr. President, why won’t you talk to me,
I’ll be here in the morning still a-grievin’.

Though you now sing a different song we know this war was planned
long before you made your stand, but it’s gotten out of hand,
broken bodies in the sand, death by your command,
and you don’t seem to see the hatred that we’re breedin’.
Your so-called war on terror just doesn’t justify
the mothers here and there who cry, who watch their children die.
It’s past time to question why, your war cry we must defy....
the call for peace we all must now be heedin’.

Hey Mr. President, why won’t you talk to me,
I’m still waitin’ and there is no place I’m goin’ to
Hey Mr. President, why won’t you talk to me,
my empty heart is grievin’ and I’m waitin’ for you

You’ve opened up the gates of Hell and let the demons in,
but they come from us within, it’s your lies that are a sin,
and this war we couldn’t win even if we were righteous.
I’m tired of war and tired of death
I’ll stay ‘til my last breath,
I stand with America waitin’ for peace, truth and justice.

Hey Mr. President, why won’t you talk to me,
I’m still waiting and not planning to be leavin’.
Hey Mr. President, why won’t you talk to me,
I’ll be here in the morning still a-grievin’.

title: Anthem for Women
lyrics:
O-oh, say there’s no way,women get equal pay!
When we do get the job,
single moms aren’t promoted.
Birth control is now safe,
under Roe vs. Wade,
Will the rights we have gained
be so quickly out-voted?
We’re not going back there,
to the times of despair!
Revolution will start,
if our law has no heart!
No hangers, no back alleys!
No welfare mothers’ children starving!
No man’s policies or creed
will make us again slaves!

title: World Heat
lyrics:
(chorus)We’ve got (clap clap) ooo-world heat!
We’ve got (clap clap) ooo-world heat!
We’ve got (clap clap) ooo-world heat!
But we’re hooked on oil, and don’t know how to quit.

I’m more than just disgusted
The climate is disrupted
     The polar ice is melting fast
The storms are so terrific
Goodbye South Pacific
     And old Bangladesh……will never last!

(chorus)

We watch the weather worsen.   (gesture left)
The farmers are a-cursin’
     The drought is doing in their crops.
But there it’s raining badly          (gesture right)
With floods rising madly
     Till the water hides……streets and car tops!

(chorus)

But look at the horizon
Windmills are a-risin’
     The blades are goin’ round and round.
And over in the ocean
The tides’ steady motion
     And that old sun……keeps shinin’ down.

(chorus)
We’ve got (clap clap) ooo-world heat!
We’ve got (clap clap) ooo-world heat!
We’ve got (clap clap) ooo-world heat!
We gotta find a way      	  to change the way we live.
We’re gonna find a way   to change the way we live.
Yeah, we’ll find a way     to
Change
        The
Way
     We
           Li-i-i-i-ive.
YEAH!!!!

title: Homeless - where is their warm?
lyrics:
When we think of our homesWe think warm and we think peace
We think comfortable soft beds to li-ie down
While outside winter storms, winter winds lash the streets
Where the bodies of the homelss willbe found
Where is their warm?
Where are their beds?
Where is their peace (where is their peace?)
Limousines pa-rk where the homeless died.
2) It is a lie that our streets
Are the homes of CHOICE to some
Who would otherwise have warm & peaceful rooms
They are the poor, they are the weak,
They are the sick, they are the lost
They are our brothers and our sisters in the cold
3) Drop a quarter, maybe more
In a can or hat or hand
Then we go-o on our way to our homes
When we need a little RAGE
Take some courage, take a stand
There'll be no more homeless dying in our town.
CHORUS

title: When We've Gotta Stand Up
lyrics:
I'll be with youTo make our voices heard
Together reaching
All people's of the world
Changing is not
Something that's bought
It's Standing, Speaking Time
2) What a wonderful listening
There will be
Civil disobedience we'll see
Our pacifist ways
Earn nothing but praise
it's Standing, Speaking time
3) Let us gather, talk
And find a plan
To heal nations,
Joining hand to hand
Violence begone
United our song
It's Standing, Speaking time

title: World Religions
lyrics:
So you're a catholicAnd she's an atheist
And there's protestant churches, all over town
The mosque is near us
The synogogue's there
Part of our community
If you have faith, no faith, from here or there across the sea
Just remember you can always find a friend in me
Because together
We're strong together
And our world is stronger too!

title: Being Pro-Dandelion
lyrics:
Oh who was it killed the dandelion?Who told us, 'no more yellow lawns?'
Ignoring all the health it gives us?
Who made the dandelion gone?
Oh age-old remedies
In every flower I see
Throw me a dandelion now.
2) IT's a hurtin' world we live in
Fog and smog and dirty air
People poison lawns and flowers
But it's advertised as care
Oh sing the dandy song
They help us all be strong
We need dandelions now.
3) I see a bad time a-risin.'
I breathe trouble in the air (coff coff)
Thank goodness for the dandelion
what would be doe if it's not there?
Oh spread the dandy word
Till everyone has heard
We're pro-dandelion now.

title: More To War Than Just War
lyrics:
There's more to war than just warIt leaves less on the earth than before
Fathers, brothers and sisters who will not be home
Daughters and sons, now just names on a stone
Where's that home? Where's that school?
Where's that child?  (Point accusing fingers at crowd)
What was treasured is spoiled and defiled
There's music unsung,
Books and libraries gone
Yes, there's more to war than just war!
2) There's more to war than just war
It leaves more on the earth than before
Scorched fields and orchards that will not bear fruit
Rubble-strewn lanes, debris, ashes and soot
Refugees, unmarked graves, makeshift camps
Sleepless nights, fearful days, shattered plans
Minds that are broken
Hate that is spoken
Yes, there's more to war than just war!
3) There's more to war than just war
There's interests and politics, galore
Supernice targets to blast with war toys
Exciting games for the military boys
Propaganda and hypocrisy
Battle specials just right for tv
Profits will soar
As the armies need more
Yes, there's more to war than just war!

title: Who Owns The Sky?
lyrics:
All the stars have names up in the skyFrom the ancient times gone by
Will we name the warheads too?
One for me and one for you?
CHORUS

O who is it owns the sky?
Who can put the bombs on high?
Is not there someone who can say?
Children, we don't play this way?

2) First star's a wishing star, they say
Make your wish as fades the day
Then your wish will sure come true
'No bombs in space,' I ask, I do.
CHORUS
3) Let us claim the sky for all
Nations big and countries small
No bullies with their reasons why
To bring us peace, they must ARM the sky?
CHORUS

title: Marching Time
lyrics:
(Brackets indicate where you can make this song local with your own street and town names)
Well, it's marching time again
And still we need it
You can read about us women
In the press
How trouble on the night streets
Keeps on happening
and because of this once more
It's marching time.
2) Well a woman up on [Brock Street]
was assaulted.
And on [Montreal] a man
attacked a friend
Every time you walk in [Kingston]
in the evening
You can't tell how your night
Is going to end.
3) Will there ever come a day
when we can venture
Outside our doors
No matter what the time?
When a woman's just as safe
As men are always?
All we know is,
Until then, it's marching time.

title: Peacetime Recipe
lyrics:
Hey, we've been lookin'For peace, we've been lookin'
How about cookin' some of it up with me?
Hey, for the babies
We can try, maybe
To replace war with a brand new recipe
I got a heart full of hope and it's goin' on still
Let's march a little more right over the holl
There's a time comin' soon when this country will see
WAR IS NO ANSWER - PEACE GOTTA BE!
Oh hey, good lookin'
Together, we're cookin'
Plans for a brand-new, peace-style, recipe!

title: Without Truth
lyrics:
(Brackets indicate where other issues could go)Things fall to pieces
Each time they tell us a lie
[USA Starwars] - No!
Don't need it
Please see our reasons why (wave handout sheets)
Our government asks us to trust their words
Believe their latest press
Forgetting all the rest
And we've tried and we've tried
And we've done our best
Without truth, our world falls to pieces
So all of us now need to stand up tall
Tell them [NO STARWARS NOW]
This is a wake-up call
And we'll sing and we'll write
And we'll not give up
For the sake of all those little pieces!

title: I Wanna be Deployed to Afghanistan
lyrics:
I wanna be deployed to Afghanistan‘Cause who could be a better ambassador than me?
If thousands of our troups mass against the Taliban
They won’t bring a peace as well as all the grannies can.

I'm bringing home a baby bumblebee,
Won't my mommy be so proud of me,
I'm bringing home a baby bumblebee,
Now my mommy won't be mad at me!
tune: I'm Bringing Home a Baby Bumble Bee

title: Petition For Our Needs
lyrics:
(This is a petition, a prayer and quite powerful, sung - also versatile)
Oh, room room, room, room,
Room, room, room for the homeless
Oh, raises, raises, for the poor
Help us recognize injustice in the coun-try
Let us stand for peace evermore.
2) Oh, jobs, jobs, jobs, jobs,
Jobs, jobs, jobs for the seekers
O equal, equal, health care for us all
May the conscience of the richest
Now be smitten
May the blindness of our leaders start to fall!
3) O food, food, food, food,
Food, food, food, for the hungry
O action, action, action,
On these woes
A clean, clean planet for our children
What we reap is surely what we sow!
tune: LIttle Brown Church In The Wildwood (Chorus)

title: Substitute Lexicon For War Talk
lyrics:
The pacifist todayMust find out how to make
constructive substiutions for war talk
A kind of lexicon of words that used to aim
to bring down destruction on others.
2) Instead of 'bombs,' let's FEED
GIVE, instead of guns
Grandmothers stand in for soldiers
To CARE, not to kill
To listen to the world
Learn the new language of Peacetalk.
2) A VILLAGE in the hills
Is NOT a 'battlefield.'
It's homes NOT 'Objectives -For-Generals.
Their young children's LIVES
NOT 'Casualties-Of-War'
Grandmothers say NO to soldiers.
3) The pacifist today
knows what Spinoza said
'Peace is a VIRTUE. It's JUSTICE.'
Not absence of war.
Not a thousand hollow words.
Grandmothers speak peace together.

title: Gran of Many Sorrows
lyrics:
I - am a gran - of many issuesI've seen trouble all my days
I-bid farewell to sittin' and knittin'
I know that I - was born to rage (She knows that she, was born to rage)
I- am a gran - who often borrows
From famous tunes, to make a point
It- ain't to show - my wit and wisdom
But to protect - this earthly joint (She will protect, this earthly joint)
I - don't know what - will come tomorrow
But for today, my song will be
De-fending nature, peace and justice
Supporting world - community. (We will support, community)

title: Coming Down The Wind
lyrics:
(Brackts indicate where you could add a different issue)[Environmental laws] they have deceived us
With loopholes that we dumbly overlooked
[Now toxic burnings can be mixed with cement]
And downwind? Well, we guess our goose is cooked
CHORUS
Toxic burning! Waste emissions!
Oh how dangerous we know they be
But they're legal
And they're [Lefarge]
And they're coming down the wind, to me.
2) I moved way out of town to raise my children
So they could breathe the clean, crisp, country air
But now we've got a [cement plant with toxcins]
Forgive me but it really isn't fair!
CHORUS
3) I pick my issues carefully, it's my time
Community concerns, our parks and schools
I never thought I'd have to come and stand up
To comopanies that make up their own rules!
CHORUS

title: Monsters In Our Bodies
lyrics:
( On: Genetically Modified but unlabeled food)
Frankenstein - was a monster
Put together - poorly made
GM science - give our food now
Far more danger than old Frankenstein displayed!
2) Building monststers- in our bodies
Altering - genetics there
These mad scientists don't know now
Long term ends of all these foods
We've come to fear
3) While we're taking - medication
Science fiddles - with our food
Could be lethal - combinations
We don't know 'cause nothing's labeled as it should.

title: Time For The Chicken Dance!
lyrics:
(Brackets indicate where you could add your own local name)Once upon a time our [province] was
A democratic place for all of us

title: One blue Christmas song
lyrics:
This song is addressed to those in a blue Christmas for whatever reason. Know that it is okay and that there is support for you with holiday pain, if you look for it. This is not a carol nor does is speak of joyous celebration but for the many out there who cannot participate, there is need for a song like this.

Parties, laughter,
Children singing,
Friends who come to call

Another long Christmas night!'

title: Blue Christmas Alternative 2
lyrics:
The Christmas songs that everyone sings, unthinkingly, can be a particular source of sadness to those for whom the words do not apply. Especially the 'might have been' concepts of home and family. It helps those in such situations, to know alternate words to sing to these taken-for-granted songs that blare unceasingly all around us:
Wish that you had a home for the holidays?
Reach out your hands to those you see in pain
A happy family may be something that you never had
Friends can help you start a better life again

title: Handling Christmas Pain
lyrics:

We can put behiind us now

You and I are grown now

To keep those drunken Christmases
From killing us, today!

title: Proportional Representation
lyrics:
With 41 democracies on the planet, all but the US & Canada use this system, which is a percentage of the lesser votes for parties or candidates are represented in the makeup of the elected government (with a threshold for how low it goes). In the US & Canada, it's winner take all - anything over 50%. So the actual will of the people isn't considered.
