title: O Lovely City, Our Sweet Home
lyrics: Note: originally written as 'O Lovely City, Madison' -- I made it generic for use by other gaggles. O lovely city, our sweet home Your golde-en promise gleams; Your city lights so clear and bright Encoura-age hopes and dreams. Yet in your streets are sleeping The homeless and the poor -- Shiv’ring with cold and fears untold They must not be ignored! For we’re one human fam-i-ly And canno-ot look away Harm done to one hurts ev’ryone Our work be-egins today! O may each winter morning Proclaim a new rebirth -- Justice and opportu-ni-ty With peace to all on earth!

title: We Wish You Happy Holidays - Multicultural Version
lyrics: We wish you a Merry Christmas Warm Solstice and Happy Hanukkah A most meaningfu-ul Kwanzaa And Boxing Day too! **see note below about other holidays Good tidings we bring, good cheer we all sing! We wish you a happy holiday And a tranquil New Year! Whateve-er the-e date is In all the-e wo-orld’s places A seaso-on of justice Is our wish for you! Good tidings we bring, good cheer we all sing! We wish you a happy holiday And a tranquil New Year! We all love our celebrations Special foods a-and li-ibations Across cultures a-and nations May peace come to you! Good tidings we bring, good cheer we all sing! We wish you a happy holiday And a tranquil New Year! **Other greetings might be inserted in the first stanza, for example Shubh Diwali (“Shoob dee-vah-lee”; Eid Mubarak (“Eed moo-bahr-ak”); Ramadan Kareem or Ramadan Mubarak; or Blessed Isra & Miraj. But care must be taken to only include these when they fall within the month you will be singing! The Muslim and Hindu calendars are lunar/lunisolar and do not follow the 12-month cycle most of us are used to. As of this writing, none of the above-mentioned festivals will fall in December until at least 2027. However well-intentioned, it would be insensitive to sing about them at the wrong time of the year.
tune: We Wish You a Merry Christmas

title: Deck the War Halls
lyrics: What will you get for little Suzie? Fa la la la la, la la la la. Screw the dolly! Get an Uzi! Fa la la la la, la la la la. And for your dear baby son, Fa la la, la la la, la la la. Why not buy a killer gun? Fa la la la la, la la la la. War toys teach your kids to kill, Fa la la la la, la la la la. They’ll learn that shooting’s quite a thrill, Fa la la la la, la la la la. Don’t buy war toys for the kiddies, Fa la la, la la la, la la la. Heed the words of Granny’s ditty, Fa la la la la, la la la la.

title: Peaceful Christmas
lyrics:
I’m dreaming of a peaceful Christmas
One where the bombs of war are still,
Where the children are living
And we’re not giving them toy guns and teaching them to kill....

I’m dreaming of a peaceful Christmas
Here and in lands so far away,
For our days to be merry and bright
We must all say “No” to war tonight.

title: Silent Night, Peaceful Night
lyrics: Silent night, peaceful night, No more guns, no more fights. No more drugs for our daughters and sons, Jobs, not poverty, for everyone. Then we’ll sleep in peace, then we’ll sleep in peace. Silent night, peaceful night, Love the kids in our sight. No more shooting or killing nearby, No more children afraid that they’ll die. Then we’ll sleep in peace, then we’ll sleep in peace. Silent night, peaceful night, Let’s teach kids that killing’s not right. Don’t buy war toys or violent games. Guns aren’t playthings, they kill and they maim. Let’s not buy any war toys, then we can all sleep in peace. Silent night, peaceful night, Lots of food, that’s what’s right. Warm bright houses and medical care, Clothes, employment, and good child care. Then we’ll sleep in peace, then we’ll sleep in peace.

title: GI Joe
lyrics: GI Joe, don’t you know, war is not the way? War toys only teach our kids the violent way to play. Oh! GI Joe, don’t you know, war is not the way? Let’s teach our kids to live in peace ’cause killing’s not okay. Little kids will learn by what their parents do, So when you buy their toys, it’s really up to you.... If you buy them guns, you teach them how to kill, But violence is not a game and we have had our fill. GI Joe, don’t you know. war is not the way? War toys only teach our kids the violent way to play. Oh! GI Joe, don’t you know, war is not the way? Let’s teach love and peace instead by what we buy today. You can buy them books, and dolls and cars that run. There’s lots of things that you can get, but they don’t need a gun! You can give them hugs and teach them how to play So they’ll grow up all safe and sound and not get blown away! GI Joe, don’t you know, war is not the way? War toys only teach our kids the violent way to play. Oh! GI Joe, don’t you know, war is not the way? Let’s teach love and peace instead by what we buy today.

title: Shooting, Shooting, Shooting
lyrics:
I need to buy a present to give my little boy.
I’ll buy him a toy soldier ’cause that’s a real cute toy.
It comes with a great uniform, and with a toy gun, too,
So he can learn to shot and kill,
’Cause that’s the thing to do.

Oh, shooting, shooting, shooting!
Now isn’t that such fun?
And when he gets much older
He’ll shoot a real live gun.
Oh parents, please consider
Before you shop today.
And don’t buy guns or war toys!
That’s not how kids should play.

I need to buy a present to give my little girl.
I’ll buy her a toy soldier with a rifle she can twirl.
She’ll learn to march in quarter time
and follow the commands,
So she’ll be set to go to war with a rifle in her hands.

Oh, shooting, shooting, shooting!
Now isn’t that such fun?
And when she gets much older
She’ll shoot a real live gun.
Oh parents, please consider
Before you shop today.
And don’t buy guns or war toys!
That’s not how kids should play.

title: I'm Dreaming of a Quiet Christmas (in the Middle East)
lyrics: CAUTION: this song is not in the public domain. It contains some elements of parody, and “may” be protected as such. That is a personal opinion, not a legal one. I'm dreaming of a just New Year For Israel and Palestine, Where no rockets glisten And leaders listen And there is hope for humankind .. I'm dreaming of a sweet season When senseless wars forever cease; May our faith and good will increase, And may all … our … years Be blessed with peace.

title: Up at the North Pole
lyrics: Up at the North Pole, Santa sees Elves are sick, they cough and wheeze. They need medicine for their ills, But tariffs raised the cost of pills. Ho, ho, ho! How’s your health? Ho, ho, ho! Depends on wealth. If you’re rich, you can be sure, You will always find a cure. Santa’s without his working elves. They have got to rest themselves. He’ll have to purchase a zillion toys To fill his sleigh for the girls and boys. Ho, ho, ho! Costs are high. Ho, ho, ho! Santa asks why. Billionaires want tax cuts now. Santa will muddle through somehow. Down near the border, reindeer pause. Strange masked men have Santa Claus. Children are waiting in every state, Hoping Santa won’t be late. Ho, ho ho! What do you know? Ho, ho, ho! ICE said “Go Back to the North Pole, old St. Nick/ We’ll keep the gifts. Now, get out quick!” This song is just a fairy tale. Santa’s love will never fail. He and his reindeer cross the miles, Bringing gifts all wrapped in smiles. Ho, ho, ho! Now it’s time Ho, ho, ho! To end this rhyme. Look at your children, hold them tight. Love has the power to make things right.

lyrics:
Our <u>bro</u>ken hearts rage

(When they throw) <u>mi</u>grants on a bus

<u>White</u> supremacists
Who have <u>strayed</u> from the values we embrace.

De<u>port</u>ed for no crime;

<u>Starved</u> and beaten too,
With<u>out</u> due process, empathy, or grace.

To <u>rise</u> and spread the truth

title: Strip-Mining All Human Knowledge
lyrics: The Billionaire A I guys have now all become convinced that it's OK to strip mine... all human achievement and sell it back to us because they think that they are gods! They think that it is ethical for them to act like hogs! They like to think they're self-made, but their models are built on at least ten thousand years of every thing humans have done. They just don't get it that the fate of each and every one depends upon all life on Earth with all its connections. They're using A I to replace... everyone who works. They think they are superior, which makes them act like jerks. Their appetites for money and the power it conveys are leading into chaos that some call "end of days!" No matter how diff'rent we look, or how we say our name, our lives all have the same value, despite vast wealth or fame! Let's stop these narcissists from using A I for control. Demote A I to tools again, 'cause that's their proper role! Demand our politicians act to regulate A I. And stop voting for Billionaires or candidates they buy! Vote only for pol-i-ti-cians who don't take corporate funds. If we don't, they'll use A I to replace ev'ryone!

title: We Raise Our Voice
lyrics: Verse 1 United States, we raise our voice, In a warning, clear and plain: This president is dangerous, To our freedom’s reign. Verse 2 He breaks the rules the world once shared, The laws for common good; A sovereign land he overthrew, For oil — because he could. Verse 3 He crowns himself above the law, An emperor so bold; No one should claim the world as theirs, For power, bought and sold Verse 4 How can we fund a distant war While needs cry out at home? With children hungry every day And healthcare we can’t pay. Verse 5 O Congress, hear the people’s plea, How long will you delay? When will enough be truly enough? Stand up. Act now. Today.

title: We Will Rise Around this Mountain
lyrics:

He kills and maims, yet he's in power still

This ego driven endeavor

We need to learn from all our history

We need better world leaders
Not these damn thugs and these cheaters
Time to rise and fight for our Democracy!

(slowly)

title: We Sing Their Names (REVISED With Names Added)
lyrics: We Grannies grieve and mourn today; It seems our nation's lost its way. It's sending thugs into our towns Where they shoot unarmed people down. These migrants sought a better life, Seeking harbor from their strife. But ICE has stripped their lives away, And so we say their names today: [Read names from list attached] We say their names and cry in pain, To see it happen once again. Now Renee Good, forever stilled. By thugs from ICE who came to kill. She went to witness in her town, When goons from ICE just shot her down. A wife and mother and unarmed; Just thirty-seven, now she’s gone. Her name was Renee Nicole Good, Shot in cold blood in her neighborhood; So now we sing and rage and shout And say to ICE: “Get the fuck out!” And though our hair has turned to gray, We sing for all we’ve lost today. In this country built on lies and shame, We mourn those lost and sing their names. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Genry Ruiz Guillén Maksym Chernyak Juan Alexis Tineo-Martinez Brayan Garzón-Rayo Nhon Ngoc Nguyen Marie Ange Blaise, Abelardo Avellaneda Delgado Jesus Molina-Veya Johnny Noviello Isidro Pérez Tien Xuan Phan Chaofeng Ge Lorenzo Antonio Batrez Vargas Oscar Rascon Duarte Santos Banegas Reyes Ismael Ayala-Uribe Norlan Guzman-Fuentes Miguel Ángel García Medina Huabing Xie Leo Cruz-Silva Hasan Ali Moh’D Saleh Josué Castro Rivera Gabriel Garcia Aviles Kai Yin Wong Francisco Gaspar-Andrés Pete Sumalo Montejo Shiraz Fatehali Sachwani Jean Wilson Brutus Fouad Saeed Abdulkadir Delvin Francisco Rodriguez Nenko Stanev Gantchev

lyrics:

tracking and growling and roaring and howling

kidna-apping fo-olks... from towns.

they'll shoot just to wa-atch... you fall!

which is used to track folks down

stop acting like the-ey're... trained mice!

title: Help Us Understand (Granny Version)
lyrics: We’ve been travelin ‘round the sun and seen what our country’s done, And it seems to us they can’t stop makin’ war; We remember ’45; they burned Hiroshima alive. There’s been so many wars we hardly can keep score. Now tell us… How did our oil get underneath their soil, And why do they think war will be the answer? Well, they marched into Korea and it made us tear our hair To think about the people dying there; Then we learned what war was for: how they used the young and poor To ring up profits for those friggin’ billionaires. So tell us… How did our oil get underneath their soil, And why do they think war will be the answer? Then they went to Vietnam, killing children with Napalm; Agent Orange killed what they had left behind. The Bay of Pigs, El Salvador, and a war called Desert Storm; It seemed we had to be protesting all the time. Now tell us… How did our oil get underneath their soil, And why do they think war will be the answer? Afghanistan was next, and it really made us vexed To see them building bases in Iraq; And we knew that they were lyin’ while so many folks were dyin’ So we marched and chanted “Bring all our troops back.” Now tell us… How did our oil get underneath their soil, And why do they think war will be the answer? Well, it’s worse than ever now; they’re starting new wars every month, And they expect us to believe them when they claim That Venezuela is a threat, and Greenland might be next, And this government is totally insane! So tell us… How did our oil get underneath their soil, And why do they think war will be the answer? We’ll, we’ve worked for all our years through the pain and through the tears To stop these bloody wars by US hands. And we’re glad you’ll hear our song, and even sing along, And perhaps you’ll help us finally understand: So tell us… How did our oil get underneath their soil, And why do they think war will be the answer? Melody can be heard at: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-VfS5sjjlm0
tune: Original Music by Vicki Ryder (c) at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-VfS5sjjlm0

Her name was Renee Nicole Good, She did all that she could; So now we sing and rage and SHOUT And say to ICE: “GET OUT!” Now though our hair has turned to gray, We sing the lost today. In our country, such lies, such shame, We mourn and sing her name. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Genry Ruiz Guillén Maksym Chernyak Juan Alexis Tineo-Martinez Brayan Garzón-Rayo Nhon Ngoc Nguyen Marie Ange Blaise, Abelardo Avellaneda Delgado Jesus Molina-Veya Johnny Noviello Isidro Pérez Tien Xuan Phan Chaofeng Ge Lorenzo Antonio Batrez Vargas Oscar Rascon Duarte Santos Banegas Reyes Ismael Ayala-Uribe Norlan Guzman-Fuentes Miguel Ángel García Medina Huabing Xie Leo Cruz-Silva Hasan Ali Moh’D Saleh Josué Castro Rivera Gabriel Garcia Aviles Kai Yin Wong Francisco Gaspar-Andrés Pete Sumalo Montejo Shiraz Fatehali Sachwani Jean Wilson Brutus Fouad Saeed Abdulkadir Delvin Francisco Rodriguez Nenko Stanev Gantchev
songwriter: Original Lyrics: Vicki Ryder. Modifications to fit Amazing Grace, Pamela Blyth
gaggle: Multiple (Triangle, El Dorado County West Slope)

title: Why Don't We All Share in the Fruits of AI ?
lyrics: Why don't we all share in the fruits of AI so workers are actually spared from working long hours until they die? Doe-oesn't anyone care? Do you remember the Jetson fam'ly where robots did all menial tasks? What would make that a real-i-ty? That is what we have to ask! AI is not fundamentally bad. The problem is how it is used. As long as employers refuse to share, workers will still be abused! Since human nature is deeply flawed with longing to be the big cheese, here is the way to make working life fair. Make all employers, employees! Workers who own their own com-pa-nies share all the fruits equally. They de-cide who should get paid how much, and THAT is DE-MO-CRA-CY! DEMOCRACY.... NOT CAPITALISM! CO-OPERATIVES.... NOT CORPORATIONS!
tune: Where, Oh Where Has My Little Dog Gone

title: ICE MUST THAW! BUT SAVE THE RULE OF LAW!
lyrics: Peaceful protests... that's the way... we live to fight... a-nother day! Organize a huge crowd who will all shout out loud ICE MUST THAW! BUT SAVE THE RULE OF LAW! Have a lot of...... people who just... vi-de-o... so others know! That is how to make sure that the truth will endure... NOT be replaced... or e-rased! Vi-deo all angles, Don't let truth be mangled! Stay peaceful! Resist the pull of becoming physical, even when they're brutal! ICE MUST THAW! BUT SAVE THE RULE OF LAW!

title: CAPITALISM IS NOT WHAT YOU THINK!
lyrics: Capital is just word that simply means money. So, cap'talism is an i-deolo-gy in which those who are wealthy....(pause)... always get their way. It lets the rich get richer and the rest of us must pay! Social, on the other hand, is based on old Latin. And guess what that word means...(pause)... it simply means "friend." So, socialism is an i-deolo-gy with a goal of everyone being treated equally! That's why we now find ourselves in the mess we're in. Fooled into thinkin' cap'tal-is-m is our friend, and even led to think that it's democracy, although the two have zero com-pat-i-bil-i-ty! Socialism is the way that we can remain free. If we want to achieve and sustain democracy, it must start in workplaces all o-ver this country. Workers must become the owners of their companies. CO-OPERATIVES... NOT CORPORATIONS!

title: Hit the Road, ICE
lyrics:
Hit the road, ICE
and don’t ya come back no more, no more, no more, no more!
Hit the road, ICE, and don’t ya come back no more

Now fascist mercenaries roam our streets
hunting down people as if we’re meat
A body hits the ground and before it cools,
they’re smearing the victim as a terrorist tool

Their approach is to kill you twice:
with bullets and again with lies

Hit the road, ICE
and don’t ya come back no more, no more, no more, no more!
Hit the road, ICE and don’t ya come back no more

They’re grabbing good people while families scream
Terror takes over and haunts our dreams
Invading clinics and schools and homes
Turning our cities into grim war zones

Now they coldly reload their heat
They’re counting on immunity

Hit the road, ICE
and don’t ya come back no more, no more, no more, no more!
Hit the road, ICE, and don’t ya come back no more!

Their puppy-killer boss has a job to do:
take revenge on cities that voted blue
With her cowboy hat and balloon-lips pout
Hand her a mic, and the lies pour out

Gotta shut down DHS!
It’s nothing but a rotten mess!

Hit the road, ICE
and don’t ya come back no more, no more, no more, no more!
Hit the road, ICE, and don’t ya come back no more!

Hit the road, ICE
and don’t ya come back no more, no more, no more, no more!
Hit the road, ICE, and don’t ya come back no more
don’t ya come back no more
don’t ya come back no more!

title: DEMENTIA
lyrics: Every time he babbles, the people wonder what’s the matterEvery time he speaks he sounds as if he’s not all thereThough he keeps on saying he really, really is a geniusHe is clearly failing, but so far he is unaware Dementia —eats at his brainDementia — It’s clear and plainDementia — makes him insane Every time he blunders so badly even he’s embarrassed,he just has a tantrum and lashes out with rage and blameFox and GOP, they all pretend he’s compos mentisThey all prop him up with excuses, but they sound so lame Dementia — eats at his brainDementia — It’s clear and plainDementia — makes him insane Policy decisions that make no sense and harm our nationPublic proclamations that lead to pandemoniumFantasies of glory and even global dominationDreaming he’s a victim, while victimizing everyone Dementia — eats at his brainDementia — It’s clear and plainDementia — makes him insane Everybody whispers, but no one comes right out and says itPeople in his orbit, they fawn and flatter and kowtowCircling like vultures, they lie and scheme to gain advantage,waiting for his downfall to swoop in fast and grab the power Dementia — eats at his brainDementia — It’s clear and plainDementia — makes him insane

title: Solidarity with Minneapolis
lyrics: We Stand with Minneapolis tune: Frère Jacques Occupation, federalization We say no, no troops there We stand with Minnesotans We stand with Minnesotans We demand, no troops there Occupation federalization We say no, no ICE there We don’t want a police state We don’t want a police state We demand, no troops there

title: Save Our Veterans Administration
lyrics: Save Our Veterans Administration tune: (Hang Down Your Head) Tom Dooley Help us save our VA…Help us save it now Veterans need their health care, These cuts will harm us all We were a country of kindness, We were a country who cared But with these deep and cruel cuts, Nobody will be spared Service has already changed now, Thanks to MAGA’s disdain Cruelty and malice, Are their PO-licy’s terrible game We’ll not give in to tactics, That aim to make us cower We stand here together, Knowing we still have power MAHA is such bullshit, Trumpers should be ashamed They’ll go down in hist’ry …With BLOOD upon their names
tune: Hang Down Your Head Tom Dooley

title: ICE: INSTITUTE OF CRUELTY AND EVIL!
lyrics: ICE is he-ere, and ICE is there, and ICE is all arou-ou-ound! tracking and growling ... roaring and howling kidnaping fo-olks... from towns. Even if you are a citizen, ICE does not ca-are at a-a-all! You get in the way of their quota that day they'll shoot just to wa-atch... you fall! ICE is he-elped by Am-a-zon providing it wi-ith high tech AI which is used to track folks down Amazon he-e-lps... ICE spy! We need to stop using Am-a-zon, and force them to stop aiding ICE. Plan protests! Demand that Congress stop acting like te-e-rrified mice!

title: ANYWHERE ICE IS
lyrics:
I have often walked down the street before.

But I never had this sense of evil here before.

Now I find myself won-der-ing "What gives?"

ICE is now on the streets where we live.

            Oh, the souring feeling

            just to know that ICE may be near,

            an overpowering feeling

            that its evil, masked, armed agents might appear!

Ev'ry step I take, ev'ry corner turned

makes my hands and legs tremble and my stomach churn!

I can only hope our Attorney Gen

Kristine Mayes, the election will win!

            She has come out and clearly

            stated just what ICE may not do

            That's why we love her dearly

            A-and that is why you should love her too!

We believe our laws are for everyone

and that also includes those who choose to carry guns.

ICE's armed agents are no exception

when they're here on the streets where we live!
tune: On the Street Where You Live

title: Ain't Billionaires Got Fun?
lyrics: Billionaires think... they're superior... to you and I. They wanna replace us inferiors with their AI. They know that climate change will kill most of us. Their fossil fuel greed throws us under the bus!..... literally! That'll leave Earth... for them and their AIs to run! In the meantime... Trump's ICE keeps killin' anyone who won't a-gree with... what-ev-er he says he wants done. In the meantime... in between time... Billionaires got fun! Billionaires are... usin' Trump and... ICE as their tools... as puppets... and scapegoats... bein' played for fools. If no one stops 'um, they'll think they have won! But in the end their fun will all come undone! They don't get that... Mother Nature... rules life on Earth. They e-quate material wealth with their own pers'nal worth. Each one thinks his wealth is 'cause he is God's chosen o-one! In the meantime... in between time... ain't they got fun?

title: Gotta Stop the Raids
lyrics:
Someone told me now I know
ICE will come and smash your door, I know,
it’s been coming for some time
When they take your friends away
Put them where no one can say, I know,
laws no longer matter

We’re not alone, and we gotta stop the raids!
We’re not alone, and we gotta stop the raids!
We refuse to be afraid

If you’re Good they shoot you dead
ICE is cold and blood is red, I know,
vicious nazis in our time
Why should people live in fear
in a free America? I know,
ICE is home-grown terror

We’re not alone, and we gotta stop the raids!
We’re not alone, and we gotta stop the raids!
We refuse to be afraid

’Til forever, it can’t go
People rising up to show, we know
we reclaim our power now
Millions marching in the streets
Soon the fascists will retreat. We know
we will rise above this

We’re not alone, and we gotta stop the raids!
We’re not alone, and we gotta stop the raids!
We refuse to be afraid
tune: Have You Ever Seen the Rain? by John Fogerty

title: We're All At Risk From ICE
lyrics:
When ICE decides you will be shot,
you have no rights
When ICE decides you will be shot,
you have no rights
When ICE decides you will be shot –
citizen, immigrant, brown or white –
Oh, we're all at risk
from ICE both day and night

We say her name, it was Renee,
and she was Good
She cared to witness for her friends
and neighborhood
She cared to witness for her town,
though she was Good they shot her down
Oh, we’re all at risk
when ICE is prowling ’round

His name was Alex Pretti
and he tried to help
A bruiser shoved a woman,
and he tried to help
A good man didn’t deserve to die
brutally killed and smeared with lies
Oh, we’re all at risk
from ICE, we can’t deny

They terrorize the blue states for a spectacle
The cruelty and suffering’s intentional
So, Democrats, you're on their list:
Better get ready, we must resist!
Oh, we’re all at risk from ICE’s iron fist!

title: Oh Come, All Ye Faithful, Raging Grannies Version
lyrics: December 2, 2025 Oh Come, All Ye Faithful, Raging Grannies Version Lyrics: Marty Eberhardt Music: John F. Wade Grant County Raging Grannies, NM Oh come, all ye faithful Joyful and triumphant Oh come ye, oh come ye To resist this regime. Come and protest it Write letters and make many calls. CHORUS Oh come, let us replace it Oh come, let us replace it Oh come let us replace it This cruel regime. Sing, raging grannies Sing citizens of every age Sing to save our democracy. Sing for justice Sing for the gift of immigrants. CHORUS Sing now to greet it A dawning fair new era Sing to bring about kindness and grace. Sing now to raise it A world of justice paramount. CHORUS Key: D / Start on D

title: Let It Snow!
lyrics: Let It Snow!Jule Styne and Sammy Cahn July 1945 California Updated lyrics by Deborah Snider December 13, 2025 for the Grant County Gaggle of Raging Grannies Oh, our politics are frightful But the Grannies are delightful And since we have seeds to sow Let them grow, let them grow, let them grow! While the president is spiteful And his actions are inciteful We Grannies are in the know Let us glow, let us glow, let us glow! Chorus: When we finally do succeed Gratitude wi..ill be so..o sweet From corruption we will be freed Then we’ll be able to lead! When Republicans are hateful We Grannies are a plate full So, let’s get on with the show Let us crow, let us crow, let us crow! KEY: F / Start on C

title: Grannies Hokey Pokey
lyrics: GRANNIES HOKEY POKEYTraditional music Original verse & inspiration: Debaura James Additional verses: Marty Eberhardt Grant County Raging Grannies December 14, 2025 You put the left folks in, You take the right wing out, you put the left folks in And you shake it all about There’s so much Hokey Pokey We can turn ourselves around And that’s what it’s all about! You put your whole brain in You take your whole brain out You put your whole brain in And you shake it all about You do a lot of thinking and you protest and you shout And that’s what it’s all about! You put your whole voice in You take your whole voice out You put your whole voice in And you shake it all about You sing for those who’re hurting and you sow a lot of doubt And that’s what it’s all about! YEAH!!! KEY: G / Start on D

title: We Sing Their Names (With a Verse for Alex Pretti)
lyrics: We Grannies grieve and mourn today; It seems our nation's lost its way. They’re sending thugs into our towns Where they shoot unarmed people down. These migrants sought a better life, Seeking harbor from their strife. But ICE has stripped their lives away, And so we say their names today: [Read names from list below] We say their names and cry in pain, To see it happen once again. Now Renee Good, forever stilled. By thugs from ICE who came to kill. She went to witness in her town, When goons from ICE just shot her down. A wife and mother and unarmed; Just thirty-seven, now she’s gone. Now Alex Pretti joins Renee Good, Killed in cold blood in his neighborhood; A VA Nurse who did his best To help and heal, now laid to rest. So now we sing and rage and shout And say to ICE: “Get the fuck out!” Take your masks and take your guns And stay away from our loved ones. And though our hair has turned to gray, We sing for all we’ve lost today. In this country built on lies and shame, We mourn those lost and sing their names. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Genry Ruiz Guillén Maksym Chernyak Juan Alexis Tineo-Martinez Brayan Garzón-Rayo Nhon Ngoc Nguyen Marie Ange Blaise, Abelardo Avellaneda Delgado Jesus Molina-Veya Johnny Noviello Isidro Pérez Tien Xuan Phan Chaofeng Ge Lorenzo Antonio Batrez Vargas Oscar Rascon Duarte Santos Banegas Reyes Ismael Ayala-Uribe Norlan Guzman-Fuentes Miguel Ángel García Medina Huabing Xie Leo Cruz-Silva Hasan Ali Moh’D Saleh Josué Castro Rivera Gabriel Garcia Aviles Kai Yin Wong Francisco Gaspar-Andrés Pete Sumalo Montejo Shiraz Fatehali Sachwani Jean Wilson Brutus Fouad Saeed Abdulkadir Delvin Francisco Rodriguez Nenko Stanev Gantchev

title: Building Democracy
lyrics:
From <u>all</u> across the USA
The <u>peo</u>ple rise as one,
To de<u>mand</u> that Congress listens as
We de<u>mand</u> what must be done:
We all <u>want</u> a true democracy
Where each <u>per</u>son has a say,
So we’re <u>ri</u>sing up as one to build true Democracy today!

Oh, we <u>won’t</u> sit idly silent while
Our <u>youth</u> are thrown in jail,
And where <u>only</u> those with money are
Set <u>free</u> when they pay bail.
And we <u>want</u> nine judges on the bench
Who’ll pro<u>tect</u> the American way,
So we’re <u>rising</u> up as one to build Democracy today!

We want <u>mon</u>ey out of politics;
We know <u>bri</u>bery is wrong.
And <u>rigged</u> elections make us sick
So we’re <u>ra</u>ging loud and strong!
We Grannies <u>fought</u> these battles long ago,
And we’ll keep <u>ra</u>ging night and day,
Until <u>ev</u>’ry person has a vote, and is welcome here to stay.

Oh, it’s <u>time</u> that Congress heeds our call
To do the <u>work</u> that must be done:
To <u>end</u> divisive fascist laws
So we <u>all</u> can live as one.
We Grannies <u>look</u> to Lady Liberty
As she <u>holds</u> her torch on high
And we de<u>mand</u> a country free from fear, from <u>tyr</u>anny, and lies.
Yes, every <u>per</u>son must be safe and free if De<u>moc</u>racy’s to survive.
tune: Marines Hymn: From the Halls of Montezuma

title: Ode to Peace
lyrics: A song to commemorate the Buddhist monks’ 2200 mile Peace Walk from Texas to Washington DC. #Walk for Peace. During this long journey, two American citizens were murdered by ICE. Verse 1 See the monks who walk in silence Slow and steady, calm and clear. Open hands and open faces, Meeting pain with care, not fear. Step by step they show another Way to live and way to be —- Walking peace among each person, Calling us to choose gent-ly Verse 2 Hear another sound upon us: Sirens, shouting, sudden loss, Guns where there should be protection, Power taken at high cost. Lives cut short and families broken, Trust dissolved in fear and grief — This is not the way of safety, This is harm, not help, not peace. Verse 3 Every path reveals its purpose By the hurt or hope it brings, One way tightens into violence, One way heals all human beings. We decide which road to follow, What we bless or what we maim — Care that builds a shared tomorrow, Or the fire that feeds the flame. Verse 4 So let’s walk the way of kindness, Gentle steps and opens eyes, Feeling grief yet holding hope now, Listening for the truth to rise. Side by side we choose each other, Name the harm and turn away. We will not be ruled by violence — Love leads on. Peace leads the way.

title: Oh, Doug Ford!
lyrics: How many roads must Doug Ford walk down, Before he opens his eyes? How many times must the people speak out, Before he hears their cries? Yes, and how many lies must he tell today, Before he faces the truth? The answer, my friend, is blowin’ in the wind, The answer is blowin’ in the wind. Chorus Oh, Doug Ford, can you hear the call? Or will you turn the other way? The people are speaking, their voices are loud, But you just push them away. The answer, my friend, is blowin’ in the wind, The answer is blowin’ in the wind. How many cuts must he make to the schools, Before we’ve had enough? How many budgets must fail and break, Before we say this is tough? Yes, and how many promises can he break today, Before they all turn to dust? The answer, my friend, is blowin’ in the wind, The answer is blowin’ in the wind. Chorus How many times will he take from the poor, And give to the wealthy few? How many deals with developers, Will we let him pursue? Yes, and how many voices will rise and say, “We need a leader who’s just”? The answer, my friend, is blowin’ in the wind, The answer is blowin’ in the wind. Chorus

title: Are You Listening? (Provincial Government's Indifference)
lyrics:
If you're voting and you know it, clap your hands (Clap, clap!)
If you're voting and you know it, clap your hands (Clap, clap!)
If you're voting and you know it
The results will surely show it

If you're voting and you know it, clap your hands (Clap, clap!)
If the hospitals are struggling, clap your hands (Clap, clap!)
If the hospitals are struggling, clap your hands (Clap, clap!)
If the hospitals are struggling, clap your hands (Clap, clap!)

If the nurses are all tired,
And the wait times are getting higher,
If the hospitals are struggling, clap your hands! (Clap, clap!)

If the schools are underfunded, stomp your feet (Stomp, stomp!)
If the schools are underfunded, stomp your feet (Stomp, stomp!)
If the teachers are neglected,
And the students are affected,
If the schools are underfunded, stomp your feet! (Stomp, stomp!)

If the housing costs are rising, shout "Oh no!”
If the housing costs are rising, shout "Oh no!”
If the rent is climbing high,
And the people can’t get by,
If the housing costs are rising, shout "Oh no!” (Oh no!)

If the climate's getting worse, clap your hands, (Clap, clap!)
If the climate's getting worse, clap your hands, (Clap, clap!)
If the leaders won't take action,
And we're facing bad reactions,
If the climate's getting worse, clap your hands! (Clap, clap!)

If the government won't listen, raise your voice, (Raise your voice!)
If the government won't listen, raise your voice, (Raise your voice!)
When the people want real change,
But the leaders stay the same,
If the government won't listen, raise your voice! (Raise your voice!)
tune: If You're Happy and You Know It

title: Renoviction Hopes
lyrics: Started in a cozy space, Thought that we would stay in place, Then the notice came, it’s clear, They’re pushing us away, oh dear. Building up our lives, Neighbours by our side, Now they want to take it all, We’re ready for the fight! Chorus 'Cause we’ve got high hopes, Yes we’ve got high hopes We’ve got high hopes We’ve got high apple pie in the sky hopes Standing strong together, we’ll rise up, No more renovictions, we won’t give up! We’ll fight for our homes, Make our voices heard, With unity and love, We’ll spread the word! Promises of upgrades sound nice, But who really pays the price? Families pushed out, it’s not fair, We won’t let them take us unaware. Building up our dreams, Courage in our hearts, We’ll stand up for our community, And we won’t be torn apart! Chorus

title: Rent Control
lyrics: Raindrops on rooftops and tenants at ease, Affordable housing and lower rent fees. A lease that is stable, no rent hikes in sight, These are a few of my favourite rights. Chorus When the rent climbs, When the bills sting, When I’m feeling sad, I simply remember my favourite things, And then I don’t feel so bad! Good neighbours who smile and share in the joy A home that’s not taken by big corporate boys. With rules that protect us, my heart feels so light, These are a few of my favourite rights Chorus

title: Climate Woes
lyrics: Chorus Don’t it always seem to go, That green energy moves too slow, We need clean power from wind and sun, A future that’s healthy for everyone. We’ve got the wind, the sun, the light, Let’s make the shift and do what’s right. They’re drilling deep, the earth they tear, Pipelines cross lands, pollute the air. We need a cleaner future, no oil to burn, The planet’s on fire—will we ever learn? Glaciers are melting, forests are fire, But the leaders just talk, making us tire They promise the change, but fail to deliver, It’s time to act, not stand and shiver Hey Canada, it’s time to rise, Let’s heal the earth, open our eyes. No more pipelines, no more greed, Fight for the future, for the earth we need.

title: If We Had Healthcare
lyrics: Chorus We need healthcare that's just and right, For every citizen, day and night, Long-term care, we’ll take a stand, Every senior needs a helping hand. It’s time for change, let’s not delay, Affordable care for all today! From coast to coast, the call is clear, It’s time for change, the time is near, No more waiting, no more strife, A system that gives every life its life! We’ll raise our voices, loud and strong, Let’s fix what's broken, right the wrong, With unity, we’ll make our plea. If we had doctors, close at hand, And nurses strong across the land, We’d keep them here, give them support, So they don’t flee to another port. We’ll stop the burnout, ease the strain, For better care, we’ll break the chain, For everyone, let’s act so fast! If we had long-term care, We’d show our seniors that we care, Give them dignity and a place to share, Their stories, lives, and dreams. We’d support them in their time of need, With facilities that give them peace indeed, A future where our elders beam.

title: No War This Time
lyrics: Yo — one shot, truth drops, no gimmicks. One voice climbing past the system’s limits. Call it “defence,” but it’s profit and deceit, War chests fat while the streets can’t eat. Preach peace, still feeding the flame, Sell the struggle, then shift off blame. We clock the spin, we’re done with the truce — Time’s run out, now the people cut loose. Public cash for missiles while the climate aches, Broken treaties, poisoned rivers, same stakes. Gaza bleeding — history repeating, Leaders chant peace, but the bombs keep beating. They push fear; we spark hope nonstop. They build walls; we rise rooftops. Coast to coast, yeah, we draw that line — Peace in our pulse, justice this time. Funds to the fight while the Earth pays cost, Billions for bombs, human care gets lost. Stack weapons, cut aid, silence the wise, Mask the truth behind flags and lies. From Gaza streets to northern streams, Peace is the path sewn into our dreams. The youth cry out, the elders remind — Shift funds to life; realign mankind. Chorus Drop the guns, drop the greed — we own it. Shut the war machine down — don’t postpone it. One world, one spark, one climb — We rise and we shout: NO WAR THIS TIME! Cut the lies, cut the profit — expose it. Hands up high, let the whole world know it. Stand for the people, hold the line — We rise and we roar: NO WAR THIS TIME!

title: Ontario Will Rise
lyrics: Listen now, the story’s told Of a province bought and sold. Homes pulled right from people’s hands, Rents climb higher than hope can stand. Tenants trembling, fears run cold— Renovictions paid in gold. Chorus 1 Go ahead—take all your treasure, Stack it high and call it gain. But Ontario will rise together— We will break these chains. Food banks stretching, day by day, Families still turned away. Shelters full as nights turn cold, Cuts to care for young and old. Safety nets are torn apart, Yet we hold each other’s heart. Chorus 2 Go ahead—take all your treasure, Turn your back on people’s pain. But Ontario will rise together— We won’t bow again. Green lands strained and tightly bound, Fields once open, fenced around. Climate cries through broken dreams, Rivers choked in fading streams. Greed digs deep through every scar, Still the earth begins to speak. Chorus 3 Go ahead—take all your treasure, Clear the forests, drain the streams— But Ontario will rise together— We will guard these dreams. Bridge Raise your voices—stand, resist! Hand in hand, we won’t desist. What they hoard, we’ll reorganize; What they break, we’ll re-rise. Every town and every shore— We are stronger than before. Final Chorus Ontario will rise—together! Ontario will rise—unbowed! Side by side we’ll build forever, We the many, strong and loud. From the lakes to city skies, Hope is where our power lies. They may take, but we reclaim— Ontario will rise again.

title: Bill 5 Must Fall
lyrics: The government rammed it through, shut down every voice, Ignored the people’s will, denied our choice. First Nations warned them, but they did not hear, Now the land is crying, and the sky sheds tears. Forests fall in silence, rivers running dry, Bees are fewer now, and winds no longer sigh. If we stay quiet, who will speak for the trees? If we stay silent, who will guard the seas? Chorus Oh, Ontario, rise strong, the land has cried too long, For the waters, for the trees, for all that breathes. We can turn back the tide, we can right the wrong, Raise your voice, stand tall, make Bill 5 fall. They call them “Economic Zones” — exemptions dressed as growth, Where no law dares to enter, stealing youth and hope. No duty to consult, no hearings held, Just a pen in a hand deciding our world. The Nations’ rights are fading, damage has begun, How can we build tomorrow when today’s undone? Every forest cut, every meadow burned, Is a lesson ignored, a future unlearned. Chorus  Final Rally Verse  Oh, Ontario, stand tall, we’ve fought and won before, Like the Greenbelt nights when people took the floor. We can overturn this bill, we can heal it all, Let our voices rise, let the truth live on. We will never stop fighting — until our rights are strong, We will never stop fighting — until Bill 5 is gone. Final Chorus (Repeat to fade) Oh, Ontario, rise strong, the land has cried too long, For the waters, for the trees, for all that breathes. We can turn back the tide, we can right the wrong, Raise your voice, stand tall, make Bill 5 fall. Raise your voice, stand tall, make Bill 5 fall.

title: Draw the Line

title: Ode to Peace
lyrics:
A song to commemorate the Buddhist monks’ 2200 mile Peace Walk from Texas to Washington DC. #Walk for Peace.   During the same time period of this long journey promoting peace, two American citizens were murdered by ICE in Minnesota.  This song contrasts the intent of both of these events,

Verse 1

See the monks who walk in silence

Slow and steady, calm and clear.

Open hands and open faces,

Meeting pain with care, not fear.

Step by step they show another

Way to live and way to be —-

Walking peace among each person,

Calling us to choose gent-ly

Verse 2

Hear another sound upon us:

Sirens, shouting, sudden loss,

Guns where there should be protection,

Power taken at high cost.

Lives cut short and families broken,

Trust dissolved in fear and grief —

This is not the way of safety,

This is harm, not help, not peace.

Verse 3

Every path reveals its purpose

By the hurt or hope it brings,

One way tightens into violence,

One way heals all human beings.

We decide which road to follow,

What we bless or what we maim —

Care that builds a shared tomorrow,

Or the fire that feeds the flame.

Verse 4

So let’s walk the way of kindness,

Gentle steps and opens eyes,

Feeling grief yet holding hope now,

Listening for the truth to rise.

Side by side we choose each other,

Name the harm and turn away.

We will not be ruled by violence —

Love leads on.

Peace leads the way.

title: Hang Down Your Head, Pam Bondi

Chorus Hang down your head, Pam Bondi, Hang down your head and hear, Hang down your head, Pam Bondi, The voices standing near. Verse 2 You spoke of files and secrets, Of names in careful ink, But never turned to face them, To honor what victims think. Verse 3 They came with quiet courage, They came to testify, But power filled the room up, And mercy passed them by. Verse 4 You waved a book of whispers, Of rumors dark and deep, Of secrets gathered, you said, That certain eyes would keep. Verse 5 You threatened those before you, With pages held on high, While wounded hearts behind you Sat asking simply, “Why?” Final Chorus Hang down your head, Pam Bondi, Hang down your head in shame. Hang down your head, Pam Bondi, You’ve only yourself to blame.

title: House of the Epstein Crimes
lyrics:
There is a house in New Mexico

and wept behind each door

now living with the pain,
you were so young, you could not know
you’d never be the same

for help
but no one heard
Now Bondi locked their truths away
The victims now are smeared

We must expose this cover-up
of wealthy powerful men,
their cruelties, depravities,
in the house of the Epstein sins

title: The End of Silence
lyrics: The End of Silence Melody: The Sound of Silence Lyrics by Sheila Plotkin for the Raging Grannies of Madison Hello darkness, it’s Code Red Another lesson in sheer dread Because a shooter might be creeping We’ll pretend we’re dead or sleeping There’s a vision that keeps twisting in my brain Mark of Cain It haunts the sound of silence We huddle close and listen hard Was that a shot out in the yard? Is it echoing off gym walls? No, it’s coming from the upstairs halls. Then our eyes are stabbed by the flash of the shooter’s light Diamond bright Piercing the sound of silence And in my terror dream I saw Five hundred students maybe more Students running, stumbling, screaming From their wide eyes tears are streaming Children hearing shots and watching classmates drop Time has stopped There’s just the sound of silence How much longer will you grieve Children falling like dead leaves? Hear their words that they might teach you Take their hands that they might reach you We’re required to keep them safe and well What the hell? They’re dying in the wake of silence But our leaders know the score The NRA will give no more The volume of that donor warning Overwhelms the sound of mourning Hear our kids cry, “The blood of your children is flowing in the schoolhouse halls. It stains the walls.” When will we end the silence?

A-bombing we will go! Ignore those groups Who’d bring home troops. Cover up with a media show-o-o. Let’s feed the rich once more, And starve out all the poor. Church and State
Church and State

title: Clearcut the Forest
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Clearcut the forest
And kill all the fishes.
DEnude the hillsides
Against all our wishes.

We can grow more in about 80 years.

Who needs green trees?
Who needs clear streams?
Who needs oxygen?
We’re fooling around with
The health of the earth—
It may not heal again.

(quiet) If no one knows it,
(loud) Let’s expose it.
Make it go away.
Refuse to buy wood

Let us begin today!

Here a break, there a break, everywhere a tax break. We all live on a corporate farm EE AYE EE AYE DOUGH. Those pigs get fat but they don’t produce EE AYE EE AYE DOUGH. With a bank account here and a bank account there, Here a scam, there a scam, everywhere a tax scam, Pigs get fat but they don’t produce EE AYE EE AYE DOUGH. title: Cycle of Abuse
lyrics: fromThe Raging Grannies Songbook, 1993 /i> There’s a cycle, there’s a cycle, there’s a cycle of abuse, Guns and beatings, harsh mistreatings, There’s a cycle of abuse. How to break it, that’s the question For all women far and near, So all sisters on the planet Can walk safely without fear. We as Grannies, take the challenge, and with thought we do reflect, On a world full of awareness, And of mutual respect. Now all people have a role to play, In this universal scheme. To enlist all of our children To embrace the global dream.

title: Danger in the Night
lyrics: The Raging Grannies Songbook, 1993 /em> Danger in the night! We must be wary. Strangers in the night Are pretty scary. Danger in the night, Until the daylight comes. When we go out at night It is not easy On streets that have no light. Some guys are sleazy. We should have no fright Until the daylight comes. Men who push us off The darkened sidewalk Just want us to think They’re full of power. We just think they stink When try shoving us around We will not be shoved around. We will not be scared-- We must have freedom. We tonight have dared To take our freedom We will walk the street And we’ll be unafraid. Come along with us— We’ll walk together. Walk along with us— We’re strong together. This is now the time, Now it is the time, We together must Take back the night!

title: Dangerous Grans
lyrics:
<em>/em>

We might be subversive—
Throw us in the clink.
We persist in thinking
When it’s not safe to think.
We poke and we pry and then tell you
What they’d prefer that no one knew.
A geriatric menace—
We instigate dissent.

We defy their wiretaps,
We might moon their snoops,
And we shall go on meeting
If they outlaw our groups.
We’ll read and we’ll write the things we please
For we believe in liberty.
We’re dangerous insurgents—
We love the Bill of Rights.

They try to keep the lid on,
Try to shut us up.
Ridicule won’t do it,
So better call a cop.
We’ve been around and we don’t scare.
We’re here, we’re there, we’re everywhere,
Your neighbors, friends, and mothers—
We are the Raging Grans!
Your neighbors, friends and mothers—
We are the Raging Grans!

title: Deck the Halls (Nuclear Folly)
lyrics:
Deck the halls with boughs of holly.
Fa la la la la, la la la la
Do away with nuclear folly.
Fa la la la la, la la la la
Grannies in their bright apparel,
Fa la la, la la la, la la la l
Sing about the nuclear peril!
Fa la la la la, la la la la

For your attention, we are dressed
Fa la la la la, la la la la
For peace on Earth, you might have guessed.
Fa la la la la, la la la la
We will sing out all our protest,
Fa la la la la, la la la la
’Til we end the nuclear menace!
Fa la la la la, la la la la

title: Democracy, Hypocrisy
lyrics: by: Ann Smith /em> Democracy, hypocrisy— How terribly muddled their meaning. Truth and right, lies and fright— What is the message we’re sending? Freedom so precious Must strengthen and grow, Strengthen and grow forever. Freedom’s plight: Does might make right? Make truth and justice the answer. (Repeat last two verses)

title: Democracy's a Dream
lyrics:
by: Hinda Kipnis


<i>/i>

WTO controls the world—
That’s the corporate scheme.
Profit and profits and profits and profits,
Democracy’s a dream.

title: Dioxin- What a Toxin!
lyrics: The Raging Grannies Songbook, 1993, adapted (Spoken) Oh dioxin—what a toxin! It helps make our paper look just right. Never gritty, Oh, so pretty. Keeps our toilet tissue soft and white. Carcinogenics are misunderstood. They may kill us off, But it’s for our own good! O, dioxin—what a toxin! There’ve been traces found in Puget Sound, they say, In the shellfish— What a swell dish! Could be served with mercury flambé! Jobs are important, we all can agree, But who’ll pay the price Once we’ve poisoned the sea? Oh, dioxin—what a toxin!

title: Diversity, Humanity
lyrics: by: Monica Zucker /em>) Some think the air belongs to corporations And not to us, AND NOT TO US! Some think it well to merge all information, In solemn tones. But we, we'd like to hear more human voices, And honest fun, And creation, While they, they want to limit all our choices To selling cars, And pushing wars. Harken! Harken! We need real debate! The Commission Must not abdicate! Diversity, humanity, diversity, humanity! Communicate with us! Diversity, humanity!

title: Dolphin Song
lyrics:
<i>/i>

Out in the ocean in a nice warm sea,
Swam a school of dolphins, as happy as can be.
Then along came the Navy, with a military plan
To train those wild, free dolphins
To serve as spies for man.

Boop, boop, dittum, datum, wattum, choo,
Boop, boop, dittum, datum, wattum, choo,
Boop, boop, dittum, your luck is overdrawn,
Cuz you've been kidnapped and now you are a pawn.

You're going to live in pens
To keep you from the cold,
Except for two hour sessions
When you do what you're told.
The Navy and the Army have got a lot of cheek,
If they think nobody's watching,
Then they're up the creek.

Boop, boop, dittum, datum, wattum, choo,
Boop, boop, dittum, datum, wattum, choo,
Boop, boop, dittum, we've got to stop the plan,
No more dolphins captured,
Becoming slaves for man.

title: Domestic Violence
lyrics: by: Monica Zucker /em> Chorus: Violence! Violence! No more domesti-ic violence. I'm leaving here today-- I know I mustn't stay I've got to end the violence right now. Drunk last night and drunk the night before. He may be drunk tonight--I won't take it any more. I'm gonna take the kids and my things and leave the rest I've got to end the violence right now. I called the crisis line and the counseling was fine, Planned my way and have a place to stay. He's got to get help, too, ‘cause they tell me "It’s not you!" I've got to end the violence right now. Chorus again.

title: Don't Make War on the "Axis"!
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Do nothing ‘til you hear from me!
Don’t go! The stoplight is on red!
Become a master of foreign affairs-
Or are they over your head?
Do nothing ‘til you can cool down!
Don’t just go bullying through.
Negotiation is an hon’rable move.
Yes, peace is red, white and blue.
Without a war
Peace and justice could win out.
Without those bombs
Can you even doubt
We’d still have clout?

Do nothing till you hear from me!
Use strength in ways that do not kill.
Wait till we all enlist for permanent war--
And we never will!

lyrics:
<em>/em>

We’re the Raging Grannies
Joinin’ the fight,.
Doo dah, doo dah.
Singin’ out
For immigrants’ rights
All the doo dah day!


Gonna rage and roar!
Gonna sing out loud!
Singin’ for the rights of immigrants
Singin’ strong and proud.

Deportation is all wrong
That’s DOO DOO, DOO DOO,
Immigrants need health benefits
And highter wages too!


It’s time to rage out loud,
For human dignity:
ALL folks must be treated fair,
That’s plain as it can be.

Many of our fam’lies were immigrants
Came from a-far.
We understand what brought them here,
But now we've raised the bar.


Time for us to say
That immigrants can stay.
We demand their right to be here now.
That's the American way!

<em>/em>

Singing our songs.

We’re the Raging Grannies
Singing our songs

title: Dream of Peace
lyrics:
<em>/em>

We’re dreaming of a peaceful nation,
With no more guns and bombs and fear.
Where all children are fed,
And all have a bed
To sleep in in the coming year.

We’re dreaming of a diverse country,
Where all the races live in love.
May we treasure each other’s life,
And all live together without strife.

title: Dreaming
lyrics:
<em>/em>

I’m dreaming of a white country,
Just like the fairy tales we knew,
Where the whole world’s Christian,
And children listen,
And do what you tell them to.

I’m dreaming we can block entry
Of those whose skin’s a different hue.
Asians, Arabs, others—
They’re not our brothers—
They could be Muslim or Hindu!

I’m certain that our whole nation
Should always look and think the same.
If religions vary
From the ordinary
Then we should kick them off our plane.

I’m dreaming of those secret closets
Where all the homosexuals stay,
And if bashers pick fights
Gays don’t demand rights,
They just don’t tell us that they’re gay.

I’m dreaming that there’s no more homeless.
Why can’t we sweep them off the street?
They don’t get my pity
If they mess my city.
Freedom’s fine—but not on my concrete.

I’m dreaming of a white nation,
Where the majority is right.
May our suburbs be merry and bright!
And may all our neighborhoods be white!

title: Eighty-Seven Billion
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Eighty-seven billion and what do we get?
A lot more trouble and deeper in debt.
Dubya, don't you ask us cuz the answer will be
We won't sell our souls to the GOP!

We're the Raging Grannies, now listen up!
You're making a mess and we've had enough!
We're gathering steam, it's time for change,
So rent a U-Haul and start packing you thangs!

Now there's an election in two thousand four.
It's our chance to boot them out the door.
We've got the power and we've got the will,
So get out and vote the bums off the hill!

Repeat first verse

lyrics:
<em>/em>

Grow GDP
Grow GDP.
SEE the economy grow
IT’S the only show.

CONSIDer what GDP REPresents,
FRACKing and O-il spills WE lament,
TRASH and WASTE of enVIronment.
Enough is enough!
(speak loudly:) ENOUGH IS ENOUGH!

FREE market growth,
FREE market growth,
The ONE-percenter’s oath,
The ONE- percenter’s oath.

Could ONE percenters SOLVE our trouble?
EVEN that busted HOUSing bubble?
NINEY –nine STUCK in a TERRIBLE muddle.
Enough is enough!
(speak:) ENOUGH IS ENOUGH!

Grow GDP,
Grow GDP,
SEE the economy grow
IT’S the only show.

GROWTH’s the MANtra we hear ev’ry day,
SUNG to us ev-er-y THIS and THAT way;
BUT there are LI-MITS TO
GROWTH we say,

Enough is enough!
(speak:) ENOUGH IS ENOUGH!!!

title: Profiling Opera
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Oh, police have perfect vision
To distinguish wrong from right.
Their decisions are made easy—
It’s like telling black from white.

Oh, the drug war gives incentive
For a thousand stops a day.
First they cuff you, then they search you.
You’re not guilty, but you pay.

Don’t go driving in the evening
In a white suburban block
With your shiny new red auto
Or they’ll put you in the dock.

Totem carver, deaf in one ear,
The Policeman called out “STOP!”
He didn’t hear him, just kept walking,
Do you think he heard the shots?

If they stop you on suspicion,
Don’t talk back or try to run.
You could end up on a morgue slab—
“We were sure he had a gun.”

Oh, police have perfect vision
To distinguish wrong from right.
Their decisions are made easy –
It’s like telling black from white.

title: Fidel
lyrics: Gail MacDonald, Bellingham Raging Grannies /i> FI-DEL, OHO FI-DEL! How can you be such a stubborn man? How can you be such a stubborn man? FOR FORTY-TWO YEARS WE'VE HAD A BOCKADE. How can you be such a stubborn man? STILL YOU REFUSE TO SEE THINGS OUR WAY! How can you be such a stubborn man? COME ON FIDEL, DON'T YOU WANT OUR MONEY? How can you be such a stubborn man? COME ON FIDEL, DON'T YOU WANT SOME FREE TRADE? How can you be such a stubborn man? TEN YEARS, 20 YEARS, 30 YEARS MORE. UGH! How can you be such a stubborn man? TEN YEARS, 20 YEARS, 30 YEARS MORE. UGH! How can you be such a stubborn man? FI-DEL, OHO FI-DEL! How can you be such a stubborn man? How can you be such a stubborn man? CAPITALISM IS REALLY COOL! How can you be such a stubborn man? YOU CAN GET REALLY RICH AND SHUT DOWN YOUR SCHOOLS. How can you be such a stubborn man? WHAT'S THE BIG DEAL ABOUT HEALTH AND EDUCATION? How can you be such a stubborn man? YOU CAN MAKE MORE MONEY WITH A CAPITALIST NATION! How can you be such a stubborn man? FI-DEL, OHO FI-DEL! How can you be such a stubborn man? How can you be such a stubborn man? (slowly) FI-DEL, OHO FI-DEL! (slowly) How can you be such a stubborn man?

title: Fishies
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Out in the ocean in a nice polluted sea

Swam a school of fishes—as happy as can be.

We’re goin’ up the river, where we all began—

But wait a minutes, fisihes, they’ve got another plan!

Boop, boop, dittum, datum, wattum, choo, Boop, boop, dittum, datum, wattum, choo, Boop, boop, dittum, your luck is overdrawn, ‘Cause you’ll never make it up—the river to spawn.

What happened to the turbot, the herring and the cod Is happening to the salmon—     DON’T BLAME IT ALL ON GOD!
 The dams are killing salmon and they have got to go
 Before the salmon are extinct and we are full of woe!
 Boop, boop, dittum, datum, wattum, choo, Boop, boop, dittum, datum, wattum, choo, Boop, boop, dittum, We’ve got to save those fish!
 Remove those damn dams before the salmon go squish!

title: Fraud's What It's All About
lyrics: /em> Sub-prime loans go in, Toxic assets come out. Cut them all up And spread them all about. Let’s do the Hocus Pocus, Bring the system down. Fraud’s what it’s all about! You put a dollar in, Thirty bucks come out. Bernie Madoff’s game And cheating all about. They did their Hocus Pocus, Brought the system down. Fraud’s what it’s all about! Speak: Heads they win, tails we lose! Privatize gain, Socialize loss, Bail out the banks No matter what the cost. They did the Hocus Pocus, Brought the system down. Fraud’s what it’s all about. They did the Hocus Pocus! They did the Hocus Pocus! They did the Hocus Pocus! Fraud’s what it’s all about

title: Free Market Trot
lyrics:
•<em> by Kay Thode
• /em>

Market shares have risen, honey—ain’t it great!
Ten thousand million were laid off, but— that’s their fate.
Oh, free markets are so nice,
But the workers pay the price.

Jobs have been exported to a—cross the seas.
Wages there are so much lower—if you please.
Who cares if wages are down,
In your old home town.

Oh, when the workers lose, the markets— rise and rise.
Profits are the only thing to—maximize.
Oh, oh, don’t you agree
That this shouldn’t be.

Wake up, workers, now it’s time to—rise and fight
NAFTA and FTAA with all your might.
Oh, free markets are so nice.
But the workers pay the price.

So if you want the workers to have— their fair share,
Join exploited workers here and—everywhere.
Fight for what is your due—
It is up to you!
tune: Put Your Arms around Me, Honey

title: From the Prisons of Guantanamo
lyrics:
<em>/em>

From the prisons of Guantanamo

To the back streets of Kabul.

They fight their “war on te-rror”

It continues long and cru-el..

First we bomb their lands to smithereens,

Saying it’s just quid pro quo,

Then we kill the kids and wo-men

And we watch Al Qaeda grow.

Oh we lock up men in prison cells

And we throw away the key.

Then we torture and we waterboard,

Saying “They are not like me.”

From the halls of Congress in DC

And from every state and town,

We must stop these wars with all our might,

And bring all troops home now!

title: Gaggle Against Caps
lyrics: The Raging Grannies Songbook, 1993, adapted by Kay Thode /em> Oh, we’re a gaggle of Grannies, Urging you off of your fannies, We’re raising our voice, we want a new choice, REMOVE WELFARE CAPS. With all the money they’re spending On military hardware unending, Behaving like saps, With their welfare caps, REMOVE THOSE CAPS. Although it’s a tradition To blame welfare for our debt, That lie is just a fiction, Reclaim our safety net. Sooo, join this gaggle of Grannies, Get up off of your fannies, We’re telling you now, we’re angry and how, REMOVE WELFARE CAPS. We really mean it, REMOVE THOSE CAPS. We mean precisely, REMOVE THOSE CAPS. We’ll say it very nicely, REMOVE THOSE CAPS READ OUR LIPS: (Mouth) REMOVE THOSE CAPS

title: Gaggle Against Coal
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Oh, we're a gaggle of Grannies,

Urging you off of your fannies,

We're raising our voice,

We want a new choice,

NO MORE COAL!

With all the money we’re spending
On fossil fuels that’re ending
There is no clean coal,
That’s a really dumb goal!
NO MORE COAL!

We need new solutions

To clean up land and air.

Stop glacier dissolutions,

And climate change despair.

So join this gaggle of Grannies,

Get up off of your fannies,

We're telling you now,

We're angry and how,

NO MORE COAL!

We really mean it! NO MORE COAL!

We mean precisely! NO MORE COAL!

We will say it very nicely!

Read our lips! (silently) NO MORE COAL!

(Curtsy)

title: Gaggle Against Cuts
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Oh, we’re a gaggle of Grannies,
Urging you off of your fannies,
We’re raising our voice
We want a new choice—
NO MORE CUTS!

With all the money they’re spending,
On tax giveaways unending
They’re hurting the poor
Of that we are sure—
NO MORE CUTS!

Stop cutting service programs-
It’s patently unfair.
Fund needed human services
SHOW THAT YOU REALLY CARE!

Soooo, join this gaggle of Grannies.
Get up off of your fannies!
We’re telling you now,
We’re angry and how!
NO MORE CUTS!

We really mean it—NO MORE CUTS!

We mean precisely—NO MORE CUTS!

We will say it very nicely—NO MORE CUTS!

READ OUR LIPS! (mouth silently, No more cuts!)

<em>/em>

Oh, we’re a gaggle of Grannies
Urging you off of your fannies
We’re raising our voice,
We want a new choice,
FORECLOSURES? NO!

With all the money they’re spending
On bank bailouts unending,
We’re losing our homes
Because of those loans,
FORECLOSURES? NO!

SOOO, join this gaggle of Grannies,
Get up off of your fannies,
We’re telling you now,
We’re angry and how,
FORECLOSURES? NO!

We really mean it..
We mean precisely…

READ OUR LIPS…

title: Gaggle Against G.E.S
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Oh, we’re a gaggle of Grannies
Urging you off of your fannies,
We’re raising our voice,
We want a new choice—
NO G.E.s

With all the money they’re spending,
Splicing genes unending,
They’re wrecking our food
And spoiling our mood—
NO G.E.s

With all of their gene splicing,
There is no way to know
What damage they are doing.
G.E.s have got to go!

So, join this gaggle of Grannies!
Get up off of your fannies.
We’re telling you now
We’re angry and how,
NO G.E.s

We really mean it! NO G.E.s
We mean precisely! NO G.E.s
We will say it very nicely! NO G.E.s
Read our lips! (Mouth NO G.E.s)

Show them you really care!

title: Gay Pride Comes to Town
lyrics: /em> Let’s sing out for our citizens. Hip, hip, hurray! For every sexuality Both straight and gay. It is just fine for us to see And celebrate diversity. (pause) And we’ll all feel gay when Gay pride comes to town. Get ready for a big parade. We’ll sing and shout. Because gays and lesbians are Coming out! (We’re out!) The closet’s not a friendly place. We all deserve a lot of space. (pause) And we’ll all feel gay when Gay pride comes to town. This is a cause to celebrate, A time to cheer. So please we should stop thinking straight For once, my dear. ’Cause sex-u-al-ity isn’t straight, There’s some things you can’t legislate. (pause) And we’ll all feel gay when Gay pride comes to town.

title: Get Out Now!
lyrics: /em> We went to war one day And now can’t get away. The Afghans know how to run the show. They’ve never given way. Al Qaida was our aim, And bombing was our game. Al Qaida fled, our face was red. Our strategy was lame. Chorus: They think you can't stop war Until you’ve won it. Once you’ve begun it You’ve got to stay. But we can’t win a war against the Afghans, Why are we fighting them anyway? The warlords are in charge. Corruption is at large. Once we invade the plot is laid They counter every raid. The lesson we ignore Was Vietnam before. Let’s get out now, let’s take a vow— Why should we ask for more? Chorus Yell: GET OUT NOW!

title: Getting Together
lyrics:
<em>/em>

What's been preventing
The world from getting
Together as friend to friend?
Could it be language?
We'll learn to translate.
Might be religion--
No reason to hate..

Couldn't be color
With all of the wonderful
Shades of the human skin?
Let's be forgetting
What’s been preventing
And invite the whole world in!
tune: I'm Looking Over A Four-Leaf Clover
review_notes: Potential duplicate of main-0046 (exact title match). Main site version from Kingston, Seattle version from Seattle. Content may differ — review needed.

title: Give Us a Beach
lyrics: /em> Lyrics: Vicki Ryder Oh, give us a beach Where oil spills can’t reach, And there’s no sticky goo on the sand, Where children can play, And the birds and fish stay Free from harm ... that’s our simple demand. Chorus: Save our seas and our sand! We’re here and we’re taking a stand! Say NO to those rigs And those comp’nies so big! Mother Earth still comes first in our land! We Grannies are blue. Our predictions came true, And their oil leaked onto our shore. It’s gooey and black And we can’t put it back. So we don’t want them drillin’ no more! Chorus “Drill, baby, drill” Was a license to kill For Hal’burton, TransOcean, BP, So boycott BP And then maybe they’ll see What we think of crude oil in the sea! Chorus

Three hundred billion is a lot of money, true, And we will get our share of it and more before we’re through. The army will protect us while we do the things we do. We know how to make war pay. Chorus A cost of war is relative and so it all depends On who are your connections and who..oo are your friends A contract here and there can do a lot to make amends. We know how to make war pay. Chorus

lyrics:
<em>/em>

GM foods are good for me—
So say government agencies.
Sci-en-tists there do agree
GM foods won’t hu-urt me.


Chorus:
Yes, scientists love me.
Yes, governments love me.
Yes, biotech loves me.
Spin doctors tell me so.

GM corn kills butterflies.
What are other GM lies?
Terminator, can’t you see,
Is good for In-di-a and me.

Chorus

(Halifax Grannies)
tune: Yes, Jesus Loves Me, to be sung in little girl voices

lyrics: /em> Who needs mammograms this year? Planned Parenthood, we do fear, Misuses funds we hold so dear. The wars on women rage. Chorus: Maybe we just won’t have sex Won’t have sex, won’t have sex Maybe we just won’t have sex, And then we’ll see what’s next. Parenthood must not be planned, Contraception must be banned, Have more kids, we understand, The wars on women rage. Chorus Pregnant once again this year, How far along does it appear? We’ll probe your wombs to make it clear! The wars on women rage. Refrain: Goodbye Boehmer! Goodbye Cantor! Goodbye, Mittens, We’re going to leave you now! Chorus

title: Got Gas?
lyrics: adaptation by Laurie Rostholder of song by Glen Anderson The Marine Hymn From Iraq and Venezuela The Arabian and Caspian Seas, We will pump out all the planet’s oil Just to fuel our SUVs. We can get ten miles per gallon As we guzzle gasoline. Since we’ll always need a lot more oil, We’ll just call out the Marines. We will pave the way for Texaco, For Standard, ARCO, too, And Exxon, Shell and AMOCO— But the cost is paid by you. And some foreigners will lose their land And their lives when we invade, But WE’LL get gas for our guzzling cars And enemies that we’ve made. From Honduras for United Fruit, To anywhere for our banks, We will conquer anyplace, anytime, Until we run out of tanks. So we cultivate our image As we dominate the skies— Not to protect rights and values, But big business enterprise!

title: Grandma's Fate
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Will they pull the plug on Grandma?
Will they pull the plug one day?
That is just what I’ve been hearing,
If Obama has his way!

If that is what you believe, dear
I’ve something here for sale:
(Speak) The Brooklyn Bridge
The Eiffel Tower
And Buckingham Palace, too!

So, my friend, I need to ask you
To listen to the TRUTH:
Grandma’s really safe
As safe as she can be.
The choice is up to her, you see!

(Speak)
WE ALL HAVE A CHOICE!
tune: I’ve Been Working on the Railroad

title: Grannies Against Stuff
lyrics:
<em>/em>

We’re the Raging Grannies
 And we’re asking you today
 To cut down on buying more stuff—
 Show your kids a better way.

 We don’t like landfills
 Full of stuff that’s thrown away,
 We like walks and talks and constructive games
 To fill our grandkids' day.

They don’t need all those presents
 To fill their lives with cheer.
 They really want your love and time
 Throughout the coming year.

 So join the Raging Grannies:
 Boycott all that stuff.
 Today is buy nothing day—
 We all have enough.

title: Grannies Against War Toys
lyrics:
<em>/em>

We’re the Raging Grannies
And we’re asking you today
Please don’t buy destructive toys
For the kids on holidays.

We don’t like war toys
And tanks and army gear
We like peaceful and constructive games
For our grandkids we hold dear.

The violence and hatred
It really must cease
And children can learn early on
That its better to THINK PEACE

Join the Raging Grannies
In working every day
For peace on Earth, goodwill to all
And singing on our way.

title: Granny with an Attitude
lyrics: from the E-Vine Rap I'm a Granny with an attitude and I'm a rapper too. I have a little song I want to rap for you. I have grave concern about the feminine form, ‘Cause the fashion world dictates emaciation's the norm. Paintings and movies say it wasn't always so. Check out Ruben's full figured women and Marilyn Monroe. Then along came Twiggie and the skinny look was in. Women have been paying ever since to be thin. We've got bulimia, anorexia, exercise and diet, Money to be spent on gadgets that defy it. Then, there's girdles, liposuction, tummy tucks and stays And if that's not enough, there's so many other ways But, I say, whether you're old, young, short tall, voluptuous or thin Be proud of who you are! Don't let those fashion gurus win!

title: Groped by the TSA
lyrics: /em> We were groped by the TSA, honey, honey, We were groped by the TSA, dear dear, We were groped by the TSA First the front and then the rear Do you feel more secure? No dynamite in our underwear, honey honey, No explosives in our shoes, dear dear, Just some metal in our knees Makes that buzzer go whee whee, Do you feel more secure? Touched our boobs, both of them, honey honey, Groped our crotches up both legs, dear dear, Doesn’t really feel that great As good lovers (speak) they don’t rate Do you feel more secure? Got felt up by the TSA, honey honey, Did not like it very much, dear dear, Maybe they could improve their touch, Take a class in Swedish massage. Do you feel more secure?

Grow GDP

FREE market growth,

title: Half the Sky
lyrics: /em> We never will forget Gran was a suffragette. We’re fighting still for women’s rights, And we ain’t got them yet. But we won’t sit and sigh, Burst into tears and cry. The world won’t change with-ou-out us, We hold up half the sky. When you want things changed Call in your sisters, Call in your mothers, Call in your grannies! When you want things changed Call in a woman, Because we hold up half the sky. When you want things changed Call in your sisters, Call in your mothers, Call in your grannies! When you want things changed Call in a woman, Because we hold up most of the sky.

title: Hanford Victory Party (January 28, 2002)
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Now that Fast Flux is ended,
 Now we have won at last,
 We celebrate our victory
 Cause that dire threat has passed.
 We thank everyone in this room
 For all the work they’ve done,
 Now we must all work to see that
 Cleanup gets completely done.

We still have a federal budget
 That shortchanges people’s health
 And they are still squandering money
 On missiles that take so much wealth.
 We’ll keep demanding more dollars,
 We will go on with the fight,
 ‘Til they’ve finished the cleanup
 And the job is done right.
tune: Now That the Ball Is Over

title: Happy Birthday Shirley 2012
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Shirley, Shirley, give us your answer true,
 How can we grow o-old just like you?
 We want passion just like you’ve got,
 And we want to be hot.
 We won’t take less.
 You effervesce.
 We wa-ant to be like you!

Maybe Shirley you-oo will tell us all,

How you manage to completely enthrall.

Your picture’s in the papers,

From so many of your capers.

You know everyone,

You phenomenon.

We wa-ant to be like you!

You do so many ki-inds of different things,
 Belly dancing, acting, and of course you sing.
 You took the stage in New York,
 And boldly invaded their bulwark.
 We do love to see,
 That Nor-wegian movie,
 We wa-ant to be like you!

You like Doc Martin and lots of serious books,

Economics and how the bankers are crooks.

Of course you don’t have strong opinions,

Just ask all of your minions.

How do you do it?

We all bene-fit.

And Shirley we do love you!

Happy Birthday!!!!

title: Healthcare Song
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Oh Dear, what can the matter be?
We all need healthcare with coverage for you and me.
We should be charged an affordable healthcare fee.
Why don’t we have it right now?

Adults and children want healthcare for everyone
Millions of people who need it find they have none.
One little illness can make families come undone.
Why don’t we have it right now?

Seniors have health care and they call it Medicare.
We can improve it and give everyone their share.
We’ll fight for Conyer’s bill, so Congress, be aware!
We must have healthcare right now.

Oh Dear, what can the matter be?
We all need healthcare with coverage for you and me.
We should be charged an affordable healthcare fee.
Why don’t we have it right now?

We will have Healthcare RIGHT NOW!
tune: Oh, Dear, What Can the Matter Be?
songwriter: Corinne Willinger of the NYC Raging Grannies

Chorus:
We still want a health care bill for all,
A health care bill for all,
Single payer bill for all.
We still want a health care bill for all,
A health care bill for all,
Single payer bill for all.

That does not give us a thrill.

Insurance profits up again.
Another version of the same old sin.

Chorus

Chorus

title: Healthcare- yet again
lyrics: Nancy Galland, Maine Raging Grannies /em> Healthcare, healthcare, What am I gonna do? I’m half crazy Trying to pay for you. I can’t afford my Blue Cross, And Single Payer cuts costs, So let’s defeat With feet on the street Profiteering from healthcare blues. Congress, Congress Give us your answer true, Are you crazy? What are you gonna do? If we can’t afford to pay bills, And wait until we’re too ill. We’ll lose our work Without help or perks— There’s better care in the zoo!

title: Heard It Through the Pope-vine
lyrics: /em> lead singer(s) plus background singers (the latter in italics) Ooh, you try to be true to your creed Help others in their time of need. You took a vow of poverty Left your home and family. The Church you pro-mised to obey But we found out yesterday Don’t you know that.. Heard it through the Pope-vine You nuns have got to get in line Ooh, ooh, ooh The Leadership Con-fer-ence Of Women Re-li-gi-ous Who would have ever made a guess You’re radical feminists? We need to pull you back from sin And we’ve got just the men To do it, ’cause Chorus background chorus You women have lost the path Of patriarchy and God’s wrath You put too much priority On health and social equity The pope knows what is best To lay your sins to rest. Don’t you know that Chorus background chorus Suggested intro: There’s a war on women they won’t win, Popes, conservatives, congressmen. Our patience wearing very thin. We won’t rely on as-pir-in. title: Hey, Look Us Over
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Hey, look us over, women proud and strong,
 Time to hear our voices, time to hear our song,
 Silent for too long - powerless as well
 But now the earth is crying out
 And women will respond by saying
 Let's work together - build, not destroy
 Teach your children kindness, every girl and boy
 'Cause the planet is home to all of us
 And that's the song we bring
 Hear the Raging Grannies Sing!
review_notes: Potential duplicate of main-0812 (fuzzy title match, distance=1). Main: "Hey Look Us Over", Seattle: "Hey, Look Us Over". Review needed.

title: Hinda's Birthday Song (2011)
lyrics:
<em>/em>

Hinda’s Birthday,
We celebrate today.
She’s SO serious,
But she also likes to play.
Now she’s an octogenarian,
But not a true vegetarian.
She eats some meat, but rarely a sweet,
Goes swimming most every day.

Hinda is a -
Dancer and actress, too.
Walked for miles to
See a Seattle view.
On stage with our hostess Becca,
In Playback improvisation,
They used their ima-ginations to
PlayBack a story or two.

Hinda’s family’s
Here to celebrate.
Raisa and Adam,
Their children can hardly wait
To fly up from California
To visit their dear Grandma.
Elliana is 5, Amalie 2.
Grandma Hinda is happy, ’tis true.

There’s Ben Louis,
Hinda’s handsome son.
There’s another name for him.
He is the only one.
Seattle’s Best Auto Mechanic,
But please don’t make him panic.
He won’t say “No”,
He’s thorough but, oh,
Don’t call him all at once.

Hinda joins a
Rather exclusive clan.
4 other active grans
Are octogenarians.
And next month 3 more will join us.
We’ll give them all a chorus.
For 80 is
A milestone to mark,
And Hinda enjoys it with you.

title: Hokus Pokus
lyrics:
<em>/em>

You know the right wing’s in.
